 [P40A / Maritime Expeditionary Standoff Response (MESR)]: FY26 funds increase ($1.70M) provided to support NAVIFOR efforts: ISO RRL- CTM Career Long Learning Continuum (CLLC), and NECC's
 effort: EOD content conversion. This also supports AIRFOR: The Avionics Maintenance Trainer (AMT) is designed to replicate current fleet avionics equipment in a lab environment, particularly for the MH-60R
 (MH-60R Avionics Systems Organizational Initial Maintenance Technician) systems. The trainers are intended to bridge the gap between classroom learning and hands-on experience in the fleet, enabling
 maintainers to operate and repair the avionics systems found on the MH-60R, such as Data, Video, and Audio Recorder Systems, Display Units for Night Vision Devices, Helmet Mounted Displays, Helmet
 Display Tracker, Systems, Air Navigation and Communication Systems.

 [P40A / Tactical Training Simulator for MA A School NTTC Lackland]: Funding supports the procurement of fully immersive simulators, a mock ship and pier structure which includes other equipment and
 enhancements supporting Ready Relevant Learning training for Master at Arms students.

 [P40A / Enlisted Accesion Increases for STS Rating]: The Submarine Learning Center requires an additional Submarine Common Operational Analysis and Employment Trainer (COAET) with 2 sets (8 ea set)
 Virtual Tactical Trainer VTAC with Beam Simulation For Sonar Trainers (BSST) workstations for acoustic operator individual and team training requirements. This requirement is necessary to meet the Navy's
 FY20 Enlisted Accession Plan for the STS rating. The COAET is a classroom-oriented training device that simulates the controls and displays of the latest available tactical combat control and acoustic systems.
 COAET provides practical experience of the training applications that do not require a full tactical team trainer. COAET can be rebooted to provide three distinct training modes: Acoustic Analysis Trainer (AAT),
 Sonar Tactical Decision Aids (STDA) Lab and Team Training. The Phase I COAET utilizes desktop/monitor workstations. This trainer supports team training combining the STS rating with FT Rating Accessions
 training for practical application in the team training environment.

 [P40A / CTM Career Long Learning Continuum (CLLC)]: FY26 funds increase ($1.70M) supports NAVIFOR efforts: ISO RRL- CTM Career Long Learning Continuum (CLLC), and NECC's effort: EOD content
 conversion. This also supports AIRFOR: The Avionics Maintenance Trainer (AMT) is designed to replicate current fleet avionics equipment in a lab environment, particularly for the MH-60R (MH-60R Avionics
 Systems Organizational Initial Maintenance Technician) systems. The trainers are intended to bridge the gap between classroom learning and hands-on experience in the fleet, enabling maintainers to operate
 and repair the avionics systems found on the MH-60R, such as Data, Video, and Audio Recorder Systems, Display Units for Night Vision Devices, Helmet Mounted Displays, Helmet Display Tracker, Systems, Air
 Navigation and Communication Systems.

 [P40A / Multipurpose reconfigurable training system]: FY26 funds increase ($1.00M) provided to NAVIFOR for functional requirements document support of CTT accession pathway modernization. These funds
 will facilitate the purchase of a Multipurpose Reconfigurable Training System maintenance and operations training.

 [P40A / Tactical simulators]: FY26 funds increase ($1.00M) provided to NAVIFOR for functional requirements document support of CTT accession pathway modernization. These funds will facilitate the purchase
 of a Multipurpose Reconfigurable Training System maintenance and operations training.

 [P40A / Aviation Maintenace Training Equipment]: Information Systems Office (ISO) RRL course modernization for RRL training approach will provide less burden on Fleet Commands warfighting by producing a
 balanced training focus for first tour requirements.

 [P40A / Avionics Maintenance Trainer (AMT) - Development and Design]: FY26 funds increase ($4.269M) for the need to support the development and design of the Avionics Maintenance Trainer (AMT) in
 alignment with the updated specifications outlined in the Functional Requirements Document (FRD). This is critical for associated risk mitigation, implementation readiness, and infrastructure alignment to ensure
 the AMT can be delivered and fielded on time and in accordance best practices. This includes mitigation of cost, schedule, and technical risks identified during early planning reviews, as well as necessary
 integration planning for initial deployment sites.
 The AMT is a critical element in developing maintainers who can operate and repair the current fleet's avionics equipment. The procurement is in line with modernization objectives and mission requirements,
 enabling the fleet to maintain operational readiness. Training objectives supports performance objective completion for: Advanced Data Transfer Systems, Helmet Display Tracker Systems, Air Navigation and
 Communication Systems.

 Operational Impact: The AMT will directly contribute to operational efficiency by ensuring properly trained personnel are available to meet fleet requirements, particularly in maintaining and operating avionics
 systems.

 [P40A - 2 / U.S. Naval Academy (USNA)]: The U.S. Naval Academy mission is to develop midshipmen morally, mentally and physically and to imbue them with the highest ideals of duty, honor and loyalty in
 order to graduate leaders who are dedicated to a career of naval service and have potential for future development in mind and character to assume the highest responsibilities of command, citizenship and
 government. The Academic and Professionally Accredited Enterprise Education Enclave (AEEE) system must ensure the best educated and most qualified junior officers enter the naval service. The Naval
 Academy must maintain the highest standards in academic disciplines and supporting infrastructure. Planned upgrades and replacements are vital in ensuring graduates are technologically prepared to serve in
 tomorrow's Fleet and Fleet Marine Force while supporting institutional accreditation and competitiveness with peer institutions. System supports the sole Navy undergraduate degree-granting higher education
 command. Hybrid network enclave serves as a dynamically variable resource for multifaceted teaching, education, and research activities in a blended physical/virtual campus environment similar to four-year
 civilian universities. Functionality includes collegiate-level admissions outreach, curriculum development, course delivery, pedagogical research, extra-curricular activities, and intercollegiate sports

 [P40A - 2 / Universal Wireless Telecommunications System]: Funding provided for LCM upgrades of telecom system from LTE to 5G within the Academic and Administrative buildings. Upgrades will enhance the
 new 5G services and coverage.

 [P40A - 2 / Satellite Validation Testing System]: The testing system that performs various required validation tests is past its operation life, and is in need of a refresh.

 [P40A - 2 / Metallography lab suite]: The equipment in the metallography lab supports sample preparation for a wide range of materials classes and projects.

 [P40A - 2 / Autoclave]: The autoclave was purchased in the wake of TS Isabel (FY2004). The high-pressure system failed in FY19. The computer control and data acquisition system are over 15 years old. Need
 to reinvest in the computer control/data acquisition hardware and software.

 [P40A - 2 / Electro-mechanical test equipment data acquisition and control system]: The computer control/data acquisition for most of the test machines is out of date, most of the systems were last updated in
 2004 after TS Isabel.

 [P40A - 2 / Aruba NAC Wireless Expansion]: The replacement system will facilitate faster and more reliable mission critical enterprise services using up to date and supportable technologies.

 [P40A - 2 / Aruba Switches]: The acquisition will be the LCM of equipment that is reaching its end of serviceable life. The replacement system will facilitate faster and more reliable mission critical enterprise
 services using up to date and supportable technologies.

 [P40A - 2 / Atomic Force Microscope System]: Required instrument to avoid USNA department being severely limited in teaching and research capabilities. Having the capability of a state-of-the-art AFM is
 extremely vital to the success of an ACS-certified Chemistry major, midshipman research experience, and faculty research program.

 [P40A - 2 / Bancroft QOL Wireless Internet]: Replacement system will facilitate faster, more secure and more reliable quality of life bandwidth for Midshipman in their living quarters.

 [P40A - 2 / Bridge Simulator Upgrades]: This simulator is primarily used for the various seamanship and navigation classes taught throughout a midshipmen's time at the academy.

 [P40A - 2 / Confocal Microscope System]: A confocal microscope system is a critical tool for both material science and understanding biochemical processes at the cellular level. It allows non-invasive, three-
 dimensional imaging of structures in both materials and living cells.

 [P40A - 2 / F5 Load Balancers]: The acquisition will be the Life Cycle Management (LCM) of equipment that is reaching its end of serviceable life. The replacement system will facilitate faster and more reliable
 mission critical enterprise services using up to date and supportable technologies.

 [P40A - 2 / MALDI TOF Mass Spectrometer]: This mass spectrometer is extensively used by the Chemistry Department biochemistry faculty for its unique ability to acquire mass spectra of large molecules such
 as proteins by laser desorption ionization. Allows for continued experiments and research within the curriculum of USNA.

 [P40A - 2 / Polarimetric Phase Array Weather Radar]: This weather radar instrument will provide the Ocean and Atmospheric Sciences Department and USNA with a weather radar capability that combines the
 capabilities of Doppler radar with dual polarization, or polarimetric weather radar technology.

 [P40A - 2 / IVER Ecomapper with Inertial Navigation]: The Ecomapper AUV was acquired by the Oceanography Department in FY16 and is due for replacement in FY26.

 [P40A - 2 / Optical microscope and imaging software]: The Materials lab has various optical microscopes and indenters of various scales that are regularly used to sample inspection and characterization both for
 teaching and research purposes.

 [P40A - 2 / Sustain Hopper Hall Technology]: Hopper Hall represents a Navy/Government strategic investment to produce a superior foundation in the global domain of Cyber warfare and represents a strategic
 focus to strengthen warfighting readiness and capabilities in which Cyber warfare will become an increasing factor. Hopper Hall will become an asset where Government, Industrial and Military leaders will inform
 future generations of Naval Officers to create an optimum platform to prepare and defend against this growing and evolving threat. The center will be the first building on the Yard with a SCI capability.

 Justification:
 8101 Descriptions/Justifications
 1.1 [USFFC] LIFE CYCLE MANAGEMENT (LCM) - FY26 decrease ($0.051M) due to amount of hardware and software procured in FY25 required within the Navy Continuous Training Environment (NCTE).
 Funding provides for the phased replacement of hardware and software required within the Navy Continuous Training Environment (NCTE). The NCTE Enterprise provides sustained adherence to cyber security/
 Information Assurance (IA) certification and accreditation requirements.

 1.2 [USFFC] BALLISTIC MISSILE DEFENSE (BMD) - FY26 decrease ($0.007M) due to slight reduction of software and hardware Life Cycle Management (LCM) requirements. Funding provides for Ballistic
 Missile Defense (BMD) and Integrated Air Missile Defense (IAMD) mission area Fleet sustainment training and mission rehearsal in theater, allow ships to participate in Navy Component Commander mandated
 BMD/IAMD exercises while pier side or underway, as well as enhance BMD/IAMD training objective accomplishment in current Fleet Readiness Training Plan (FRTP) underway training events such as
 Composite Training Unit Exercise (COMPTUEX) and Joint Training Unit Exercise (JTFEX).

 1.3 [USFFC] BALLISTIC MISSILE DEFENSE (BMD) FLEET SYNTHETIC TRAINING (FST) at Sea - FY26 decrease ($0.0001M) slight reduction for software and hardware Life Cycle Management (LCM)
 requirements. Funding provides for Ballistic Missile Defense (BMD) and Integrated Air Missile Defense (IAMD) mission area Fleet sustainment training and mission rehearsal in theater, allow ships to participate
 in Navy Component Commander mandated BMD/IAMD exercises while pier side or underway, as well as enhance BMD/IAMD training objective accomplishment in current Fleet Readiness Training Plan (FRTP)
 underway training events such as Composite Training Unit Exercise (COMPTUEX) and Joint Training Unit Exercise (JTFEX).

 1.4 [USFFC] ANTI-ACCESS AREA DENIAL (A2AD) - FY26 decrease ($0.001M) due to slight reduction in software and hardware Life Cycle Management (LCM) requirements. Funding provides for Gateway
 portable embarkation kits provides training capability against the high end A2AD, cyber, Information Warfare (IW), and Electronic Warfare (EW) threat during Carrier Strike Group (CSG) Navy training events/
 exercises.

 1.5 through 1.6 N/A

 1.7 [USFFC] NAVAL INTEGRATED FIRE CONTROL - COUNTER AIR (NIFC-CA) - FY26 decrease ($0.002M) due to slight reduction of software and hardware Life Cycle Management (LCM) requirements.
 Funding provides engineering support for Cooperative Engagement Capability (CEC) Interim Training (CIT) System portable embarkation kit (PEK) to support Carrier Strike Group (CSG) Fleet training
 requirements.

 1.8 [USFFC] INTEGRATED MARITIME PORTABLE ACOUSTIC SCORING AND SIMULATION (IMPASS) SYSTEM - FY26 increase ($0.527M) due to even-numbered FY funding for phased replacement of
 Integrated Maritime Portable Acoustic Scoring and Simulation (IMPASS) system. Funding provides for the phased replacement of the Integrated Maritime Portable Acoustic Scoring and Simulation (IMPASS)
 system, programmed for even years.

 1.9 [USFFC] LVC FLEET TRAINING WHOLENESS (FTW) - FY26 decrease ($0.016M) due to slight reduction of Life Cycle Management (LCM) requirements due to FY25 investments. Funding provides for
 procurement of equipment to support Fleet Synthetic Training (FST) at Sea capability for non-Ballistic Missile Defense (BMD) Aegis and Ship Self-Defense System (SSDS) platforms and procurement of Total
 Ship Training Capability (TSTC) shipboard synthetic radios required for realistic Command and Control (C2) representation in a Live, Virtual, and Constructive (LVC) environment.

 1.10 [USFFC] EAST COAST TRAINING NETWORK CONSOLIDATION - FY26 decrease ($0.020M) due to slight reduction of software and hardware Life Cycle Management (LCM) requirements. Funding
 provides technical refresh and sustainment of network consolidation equipment integrating East Coast tactical training ranges and systems with the Navy Continuous Training Environment (NCTE).

 1.11 [USFFC] WEST COAST TRAINING NETWORK CONSOLIDATION - FY26 decrease ($0.006M) due to slight reduction of software and hardware Life Cycle Management (LCM) requirements. Funding
 provides technical refresh and sustainment of network consolidation equipment integrating West Coast tactical training ranges and systems with the Navy Continuous Training Environment (NCTE).

 1.12 N/A

 1.13 [USFFC / CSG4] TRAINING SUPPORT VESSEL (TSV) PROCUREMENT & SUSTAINMENT - FY26 increase ($0.068M) provides for sustainment requirements for four (4) Training Support Vessels
 (TSVs). Funding provides for the availability, utility, and long-term viability of Training Support Vessels (TSVs) support to Multi-Ship Group Sail (GRUSL) Exercises, Independent Deployer Certification Exercise
 (IDCERTEX), Composite Training Unit Exercise (COMPTUEX), and Afloat Training Group (ATG) training events.

 1.14 through 1.19 N/A

 1.20 [USFFC] NCTE CROSS DOMAIN ENTERPRISE SOLUTION FOR LVC TRAINING - FY26 increase ($0.115M) due to slight expansion of software and hardware Life Cycle Management (LCM)
 requirements. Funding provides for classification levels of the Enterprise Network Guard (ENG) Raise the Bar (RTB) solution to be sustained across the FYDP. NCTE Cross Domain Enterprise Solution (NCDES)
 is required for unit, integrated and advanced training/certification at all Live, Virtual, and Constructive (LVC) training ranges.

 1.21 N/A

 1.22 [USFFC] WEST COAST LVC NETWORK CONSOLIDATION PHASE 2 - FY26 increase ($0.1M) due to slight expansion of software and hardware Life Cycle Management (LCM) requirements. Funding
 provides for consolidation of tactical training range (TTR) networks into the Navy Continuous Training Environment (NCTE) network (i.e., Navy Enterprise Tactical Training Network (NETTN)) to meet Fleet
 training requirements and network operations and support (communications, systems administrators, and cybersecurity).

 1.23 [USFFC / CSG4] TRAINING SUPPORT VESSEL (TSV) MAINT BASELINE ADJUSTMENT - FY26 increase ($0.001M) provides for dry docking intervals and regular intermediate maintenance as required
 by the American Bureau of Shipping (ABS). Funding provides for dry docking intervals and regular intermediate maintenance as required by the American Bureau of Shipping (ABS). Dry docking costs have
 accelerated more than inflation based on several factors: dry dock limitations (fewer interested bidders in a constrained environment), steel pricing schedules and Training Support Vessels (TSV) age.

 1.24 [USFFC] STRIKE GROUP MOBILE OPEN OCEAN VIRTUAL RANGE (SGMOOVR) - FY26 remains constant($0.00M) based upon level two-year procurement and installation of hardware and software
 requirements to enable LVC training during Advanced and Integrated Phase deployment workups and flexibility for enroute deployment LVC training. Funding provides an interim solution (via three (3) Fly Away
 Kits) for deployed Strike Groups (SG) to conduct Live, Virtual, Constructive (LVC) mission rehearsal events in preparation for Combatant Commander's (CCMD) tasking.

 1.25 [USFFC] NCTE CYBER HARDENING - FY26 increase ($9.200M) based on new program start to increase cyber network security. Funding provides for the procurement and installation of software and
 hardware solutions on the Navy Continuous Training Environment (NCTE) to align the Naval Enterprise Tactical Training network (NETTN) with DoD Zero Trust (ZT) cybersecurity requirements by FY27.

 1.26 [USFFC] SURFACE LVC TRAINING - FY26 increase ($7.886M) based on new program start to increase Surface Live Virtual Constructive (LVC) training program capability. Funding procures hardware and
 infrastructure to support increased Live Virtual Constructive (LVC) training event support to Fleet surface units at Fleet concentration centers on both East Coast and West Coast.

 1.27 [USFFC N7] LVC CAPABILITY FOR CVW-5/FDNFJ - FY26 decrease ($2.488M) due to the FY25 procurement of Live, Virtual, and Constructive (LVC) training capability requirements for Carrier Wing Five
 (CVW-5). Funds provides for support and equipment necessary to field, integrate, operate, and sustain an Iwakuni CVW-5 "Mini-EXCON" LVC training capability for local exercise control of integrated fixed wing
 aircraft mission trainers and simulators.

 1.28 [BUPERS] FIREARMS TRAINING SIMULATOR (FATS) - FY26 funds provided for procurement of shore-based Firearms Training Simulators (FATS) at Center for Submarine Learning (CSL) to reduce time
 and energy expended for small arms proficiency by personnel assigned to small ships and submarines that lack this capability afloat. These un-tethered weapons systems and scenarios place the students into
 the action and afford them the ability to freely maneuver and handle the weapons.

 4.10 [BUPERS] NAVAL EDUCATION AND TRAINING COMMAND (NETC) CLASSROOM FOR INCREASED ACCESSIONS - Instructor Podium Equip: FY26 decrease based on requirements such as monitors,
 cables, audio-visual components, etc.

 4.13 [BUPERS] NETC Classrooms for Incr. Acc. - Cable Infrastructure Upgrades: FY26 supports NETC implementing a Virtual Desktop Infrastructure (VDI) solution that largely eliminates physical workstations
 in classrooms. Under VDI, students utilize "zero client desktop devices" which provide a user experience similar to physical workstations, without the use of CPU, local storage or Operating Systems. This
 transformation effort will reduce the number of fielded networking solutions via implementation of standard equipment configurations.

 4.16 [BUPERS] TRAINING NETWORK (TRANET) CAPABILITY UPGRADES - ROUTING/SWITCHING - FY26 funds provided for upgrades support CISCO ISR 4431 and ISCO Catalyst 9410 routing and
 switching equipment. This funding profile changes are a result of multi-phased strategy for major and different components of the training network.

 4.17 [BUPERS] TRAINING NETWORK (TRANET) CAPABILITY UPGRADES - WIRELESS - FY26 funds provide for upgrades support Aruba Controllers, switches and access points for the training network.

 4.19 [BUPERS] TRAINING NETWORK (TRANET) CAPABILITY UPGRADES - NETWORK SWITCH UPGRADES GREAT LAKES: FY26 supports requirement costs for site survey and single mode fiber
 installation. The funding profile changes are a result of multi-phased strategy for major and different components of the training network.

 8.1 [BUPERS] DATA CENTER CONSOLIDATION - FY26 funds provided for sustainment of data center hardware, software, and licenses for established Component Enterprise Data Centers (CEDCs) and other
 approved hosting locations as part of the Department of Defense's Information Technology efficiency for the Manpower, Training, and Education (MPT&E) Data Center footprint. The funding enables the MPT&E
 to implement the Navy Data Center and Commercial Cloud Resourcing Memorandum of Agreement. Funding will procure servers (encompassing both hardware and software components), ancillary equipment
 associated with the servers (racks, cables, switches, etc.), and integration and installation efforts as needed to meet additional hosting requirements as consolidated applications are hosted in approved hosting
 locations, and as a part of regular data center technical refresh. Regular technical refresh is required to maintain cyber security readiness and accreditation at the data centers.

 10.1 [BUPERS] MARITIME EXPEDITIONARY STANDOFF RESPONSE (MESR) - FY26 funds increase ($1.70M) provided to support NAVIFOR efforts: ISO RRL- CTM Career Long Learning Continuum (CLLC),
 and NECC's effort: EOD content conversion. This also supports AIRFOR: The Avionics Maintenance Trainer (AMT) is designed to replicate current fleet avionics equipment in a lab environment, particularly for
 the MH-60R (MH-60R Avionics Systems Organizational Initial Maintenance Technician) systems. The trainers are intended to bridge the gap between classroom learning and hands-on experience in the fleet,
 enabling maintainers to operate and repair the avionics systems found on the MH-60R, such as Data, Video, and Audio Recorder Systems, Display Units for Night Vision Devices, Helmet Mounted Displays,
 Helmet Display Tracker, Systems, Air Navigation and Communication Systems.

 17.1 [BUPERS] CTM CAREER LONG LEARNING CONTINUUM (CLLC) - FY26 funds increase ($1.70M) supports NAVIFOR efforts: ISO RRL- CTM Career Long Learning Continuum (CLLC), and NECC's
 effort: EOD content conversion. This also supports AIRFOR: The Avionics Maintenance Trainer (AMT) is designed to replicate current fleet avionics equipment in a lab environment, particularly for the MH-60R
 (MH-60R Avionics Systems Organizational Initial Maintenance Technician) systems. The trainers are intended to bridge the gap between classroom learning and hands-on experience in the fleet, enabling
 maintainers to operate and repair the avionics systems found on the MH-60R, such as Data, Video, and Audio Recorder Systems, Display Units for Night Vision Devices, Helmet Mounted Displays, Helmet
 Display Tracker, Systems, Air Navigation and Communication Systems.

 18.1 [BUPERS] MULTIPURPOSE RECONFIGURABLE TRAINING SYSTEM - FY26 funds increase ($1.00M) provided to NAVIFOR for functional requirements document support of CTT accession pathway
 modernization. These funds will facilitate the purchase of a Multipurpose Reconfigurable Training System maintenance and operations training.

 18.2 [BUPERS] TACTICAL SIMULATORS - FY26 funds increase ($1.00M) provided to NAVIFOR for functional requirements document support of CTT accession pathway modernization. These funds will
 facilitate the purchase of a Multipurpose Reconfigurable Training System maintenance and operations training.

 19.1 [BUPERS] AVIONICS MAINTENANCE TRAINER (AMT) DEVELOPMENT AND DESIGN - FY26 funds increase ($4.269) requirements stem from the need to support the development and design of
 the Avionics Maintenance Trainer (AMT) in alignment with the updated specifications outlined in the Functional Requirements Document (FRD). This is critical for associated risk mitigation, implementation
 readiness, and infrastructure alignment to ensure the AMT can be delivered and fielded on time and in accordance best practices. This includes mitigation of cost, schedule, and technical risks identified during
 early planning reviews, as well as necessary integration planning for initial deployment sites. FY26 funding adjustments support the development of AMT specifications, including the purchase and integration
 of necessary equipment for training ($2million), which will enable maintainers to meet operational and performance objectives. The AMT will allow for the following: Hands-on training with the latest avionics
 systems in a controlled lab environment, improved training and maintenance readiness, helping bridge the gap between theory and actual maintenance work, support for the development of new maintainers to
 be integrated into the fleet for completion of the first 4 units.
 The AMT is a critical element in developing maintainers who can operate and repair the current fleet's avionics equipment. The procurement is in line with modernization objectives and mission requirements,
 enabling the fleet to maintain operational readiness. Training objectives supports performance objective completion for: Advanced Data Transfer Systems, Helmet Display Tracker Systems, Air Navigation and
 Communication Systems.

 ==========================================================================================================================
 FY26 Education Support Equipment Changes

 1.23 [BUPERS] Satellite Validation Testing System - FY26 increase ($0.600M); Student-built satellites that are manifested on space launches must go through a system validation testing regime to be qualified
 for launch. The required validation tests are NASA and DoD imposed requirements for all space-flying satellites. The testing system that performs various required validation tests is past its operation life, and is
 in need of a refresh. The system includes a clean room for handling flight hardware, satellite hardware-in-the-loop test bed for performing functional checkouts, rendezvous and proximity operation (RPO) testing
 lab setup for performing RPO validation tests, a solar simulator for solar panel tests, and a thermal vacuum chamber for space environment testing.
 1.37 [BUPERS] Metallography lab suite - FY26 increase ($0.350M); The equipment in the metallography lab supports sample preparation for a wide range of materials classes and projects. There is an arcade of
 miscellaneous units in the metallography lab, all of them are 8-10 years old (or more). This includes cut off wheels, diamond wafering saws, precision grinders, polishing units, and zoom microscope units. This
 equipment gets hard use in a dirty environment. The staff does their best to maintain this equipment, but it is challenging.
 1.38 [BUPERS] Autoclave - FY26 increase ($0.500M); The autoclave in the Materials Lab is used in a variety of composite projects, it has been used to manufacture the composite monocoque for the FSAE
 team, drone parts, satellite components for JHU-APL, even transparent armor (CRADA). The autoclave was purchased in the wake of TS Isabel (FY2004). The high-pressure system failed in FY19. The
 computer control and data acquisition system are over 15 years old.
 1.39 [BUPERS] Electro-mechanical test equipment data acquisition and control system - FY26 increase ($0.500M); Mechanical test machines in the Materials Lab are the most utilized pieces of equipment for
 course support and provide testing capability needed across a wide range of projects both capstone and research projects. A mechanical test machine is comprised of the load frame and the computer control/
 data acquisition hardware and software. The computer control/data acquisition for most of the test machines is out of date, most of these systems were last updated in 2004 after TS Isabel.
 1.40 [BUPERS] Aruba NAC Wireless Expansion - FY26 increase ($0.419M); The acquisition replaced Aruba Wireless Infrastructure equipment that is reaching its end of serviceable life. The replacement system
 will facilitate faster and more reliable mission critical enterprise services using up to date and supportable technologies.
 1.41 [BUPERS] Aruba Switches - FY26 increase ($1.298M); The acquisition will be the LCM of equipment that is reaching its end of serviceable life. The replacement system will facilitate faster and more
 reliable mission critical enterprise services using up to date and supportable technologies.
 1.42 [BUPERS] Atomic Force Microscope System - FY26 increase ($0.250M); Our current Atomic Force Microscope (AFM) System (MultiMode 8 Atomic Force Microscope (AFM) by Bruker Nano Inc.) is capable
 of multiple imaging modes in both air and fluid. AFM is an important and very useful technique for imaging surface structures with accuracy and extremely high (atomic-scale) resolution. The instrument is
 used extensively to support the Integrated Laboratory course IV (SC364), midshipman research (SC495/496/476), Trident Scholar (SC589), and Bowman Scholar research projects, as well as numerous faculty
 members performing biological/biochemical, chemical, material and polymer science, and electrochemistry research. Having the capability of a state-of-the-art AFM is extremely vital to the success of our ACS-
 certified Chemistry major, midshipman research experience, and faculty research program.

 1.43 [BUPERS] Bancroft QOL Wireless Internet - FY26 increase ($0.368M); Quality of Life (QOL) Wireless Internet will be end of service life. This replacement system will facilitate faster, more secure and more
 reliable quality of life bandwidth for Midshipman in their living quarters.
 1.44 [BUPERS] Bridge Simulator Upgrades - FY26 increase ($0.348M); This simulator is primarily used for the various seamanship and navigation classes taught throughout a midshipmen's time at the academy.
 As part of the Navigation and Seamanship curriculum every Midshipmen uses this system. It demonstrates the application of electronic navigation with VMS & RADAR and introduces and reinforces Navigation/
 Bridge Watch team proficiency.

 1.45 [BUPERS] Confocal Microscope System - FY26 increase ($0.600M); A confocal microscope system is a critical tool for both material science and understanding biochemical processes at the cellular level.
 It allows non-invasive, three-dimensional imaging of structures in both materials and living cells. The use of fluorescent labels means that specific molecules or components can be identified in a complex space
 amongst other unlabeled structures. The current microscope is reaching the end of its lifetime and there is no other system available at the Naval Academy that can replicate its capabilities. Technological
 advances made in the last 10 years have improved both the speed and capabilities of confocal systems which will expand the possible measurements both midshipman and faculty researchers can perform.
 1.46 [BUPERS] F5 Load Balancers - FY26 increase ($0.308M); The acquisition will be the LCM of equipment that is reaching its end of serviceable life. The replacement system will facilitate faster and more
 reliable mission critical enterprise services using up to date and supportable technologies.
 1.47 [BUPERS] MALDI TOF Mass Spectrometer - FY26 increase ($0.250M); This mass spectrometer is extensively used by the Chemistry Department biochemistry faculty for its unique ability to acquire mass
 spectra of large molecules such as proteins by laser desorption ionization. Allows for continued experiments and research within the curriculum of USNA.
 1.48 [BUPERS] Polarimetric Phase Array Weather Radar - FY26 increase ($0.450M); This weather radar instrument will provide the Department of OAS and USNA with a weather radar capability that combines
 the capabilities of Doppler radar with dual polarization, or polarimetric weather radar technology. The polarimetric radar will open avenues of research on a range of topics from cloud processes to precipitation to
 severe weather. It will build upon past and ongoing faculty and faculty-led Midshipman research and hands-on Capstone projects from 2022-2023 focused on weather prediction and severe weather phenomena
 leading to new, exciting, and operationally-relevant Independent Research and project-based Capstones for USNA Oceanography majors and other collaborative research opportunities.
 1.49 [BUPERS] IVER Ecomapper with Inertial Navigation - FY26 increase ($0.500M); The Ecomapper AUV was acquired by the Oceanography Department in FY16 and is due for replacement in FY26. This
 state-of-the-art oceanographic research instrument is deployed in Oceanography Core, Elective, research, and Capstone Courses to collect data for analysis by midshipmen. The relatively small size and light
 weight of this instrument makes it ideal for operations in the relatively shallow waters around the U.S. Naval Academy. As AUV's and gliders are platforms commonly used in Naval Operations, deploying,
 recovering, and analyzing data from the Ecomapper AUV ties the mids' education with professional training.
 1.50 [BUPERS] Optical microscope and imaging software - FY26 increase ($0.250M); The Materials lab has various optical microscopes and indenters of various scales that are regularly used to sample
 inspection and characterization both for teaching and research purposes. Teaching includes lab instruction in both mechanical and nuclear engineering as well as project based learning both in elective classes
 as well as capstone. This capability includes specialized digital cameras and image analysis software. Reinvestment is needed in the benchtop indenters used by students throughout the School of Engineering
 & Weapons.

1810N / 07 / 1                                                                                8101 / Training and Education Equipment                                                        Training Support Equipment
 (1)
       FY26 decrease ($0.051M) due to amount of hardware and software procured in FY25 required within the Navy Continuous Training Environment (NCTE).
 (2)
       FY26 decrease ($0.007 M) due to slight reduction of software and hardware Life Cycle Management (LCM) requirements.
 (3)
       FY26 decrease ($0.001M) slight reduction for software and hardware Life Cycle Management (LCM) requirements.
 (4)
       FY26 decrease ($0.001M) due to slight reduction in software and hardware Life Cycle Management (LCM) requirements.
 (5)
       FY26 decrease ($0.002M) due to slight reduction of software and hardware Life Cycle Management (LCM) requirements.
 (6)
       FY26 increase ($0.527M) due to even-numbered FY funding for phased replacement of Integrated Maritime Portable Acoustic Scoring and Simulation (IMPASS) system.
 (7)
       FY26 decrease ($0.016M) due to slight reduction of Life Cycle Management (LCM) requirements due to FY25 investments.
 (8)
       FY26 decrease ($0.020M) due to slight reduction of software and hardware Life Cycle Management (LCM) requirements.
 (9)
       FY26 decrease ($0.006M) due to slight reduction of software and hardware Life Cycle Management (LCM) requirements.
 (10)
        FY26 increase ($0.068M) provides for sustainment requirements for four (4) Training Support Vessels (TSVs).
 (11)
        FY26 increase ($0.1M) due to slight expansion of software and hardware Life Cycle Management (LCM) requirements.
 (12)
        FY26 decrease ($0.002M) due to slight reduction of software and hardware Life Cycle Management (LCM) requirements.
 (13)
        FY26 increase ($0.001M) provided for dry docking intervals and regular intermediate maintenance as required by the American Bureau of Shipping (ABS).
 (14)
     FY26 remains constant($0.00M) based upon level two-year procurement and installation of hardware and software requirements to enable LVC training during Advanced and Integrated Phase deployment work-ups and flexibility for
 enroute deployment LVC training.
 (15)
        FY26 increase ($9.200M) based on new program start to increase cyber network security.
 (16)
        FY26 increase ($7.886M) based on new program start to increase Surface Live Virtual Constructive (LVC) training program capability.
 (17)
        FY26 decrease ($2.489M) due to the FY25 procurement of Live, Virtual, and Constructive (LVC) training capability requirements for Carrier Wing Five (CVW-5).
 (18)
        FY26 funds increased by $190K for procurement of shore-based Firearms Training Simulators (FATS).
 (19)
     FY26 supports NETC implementing a Virtual Desktop Infrastructure (VDI) solution that largely eliminates physical workstations in classrooms. Under VDI, students utilize "zero client desktop devices" which provide a user experience
 similar to physical workstations, without the use of CPU, local storage or Operating Systems. This transformation effort will reduce the number of fielded networking solutions via implementation of standard equipment configurations.
 (20)
        Funding supports TRANET Capability Upgrades which support CISCO ISR 4431 and ISCO Catalyst 9410 routing and switching equipment.
 (21)
        FY26 funds provide for upgrades support Aruba Controllers, switches and access points for the training network.
 (22)
        FY26 increase for TRANET Capability Upgrades.
 (23)
        FY26 increase to NETC SIPR Datacenter.
 (24)
      Ready, Relevant Learning (RRL) is a transformational Navy training initiative designed to accelerate the learning of every Sailor for faster response to our rapidly changing warfighting requirements and to achieve higher performance by
 coupling the timing of training delivery with every Sailor's actual deck plate needs. RRL is developing a continuum-of-learning by offering the right training at the right time so that our Sailors are ready to operate their equipment at the extreme
 technical end of its capability to win the high-end fight. RRL is transforming outdated training content and archaic methodology into a modernized, on demand, Fleet-responsive learning system. Modernized training delivery is producing more
 efficient and effective training by leveraging new technologies and training support via several initiatives including: MyNavy Career Center, MyNavy Portal, Learning Stack, and Authoritative Data Environment. RRL recognizes the nature of
 current baseline numbers. LI 8101 maintains alignment with priority readiness goals.
 (25)

 technical end of its capability to win the high-end fight. RRL is transforming outdated training content and archaic methodology into a modernized, on demand, Fleet-responsive learning system. Modernized training delivery is producing more
 efficient and effective training by leveraging new technologies and training support via several initiatives including: MyNavy Career Center, MyNavy Portal, Learning Stack, and Authoritative Data Environment. RRL recognizes the nature of
 current baseline numbers. LI 8101 maintains alignment with priority readiness goals.

 (28)
      Ready, Relevant Learning (RRL) is a transformational Navy training initiative designed to accelerate the learning of every Sailor for faster response to our rapidly changing warfighting requirements and to achieve higher performance by
 coupling the timing of training delivery with every Sailor's actual deck plate needs. RRL is developing a continuum-of-learning by offering the right training at the right time so that our Sailors are ready to operate their equipment at the extreme
 technical end of its capability to win the high-end fight. RRL is transforming outdated training content and archaic methodology into a modernized, on demand, Fleet-responsive learning system. Modernized training delivery is producing more
 efficient and effective training by leveraging new technologies and training support via several initiatives including: MyNavy Career Center, MyNavy Portal, Learning Stack, and Authoritative Data Environment. RRL recognizes the nature of
 current baseline numbers. Each project within LI 8101 has been reevaluated due to budget constraints imposed by initial baseline cuts for FY26. LI 8101 maintains alignment with priority readiness goals.

 Description:
 This line provides for new and systematic replacement of investment items required in support of the Navy's operational mission. Procurement of Command Support Equipment throughout the Navy involves the
 purchase, replacement and upgrade of various pieces of equipment such as Cable Replacements and the purchase, repair, and upgrade of information technology (IT) Infrastructure and Security Systems. This
 equipment is an integral part of the programs to improve the fleet and shore establishment by maintaining or expanding existing capabilities or replacing ineffective units.

 The procurement of Command Support Equipment involves the purchase, replacement and upgrade of various types of equipment, to include, but not limited to, critical cable plant repair / upgrade, Voice, Video,
 and Data Infrastructure and security disintegrators / systems at world-wide Base Communications Office (BCO) sites throughout the Pacific and Atlantic areas of responsibility.

 The Shore Modernization element of this line provides Command, Control, Computer, Communications and Intelligence (C4I) installations for Information Warfare (IW) Military Construction (MILCON) projects
 and ensures a holistic platform configuration control, maintenance, sustain and modernization model is carried out for shore platforms critical to Navy IW Distributed Maritime Operations (DMO). C3 Platforms
 include Naval Communications, Telecommunications Area Mater Stations (NCTAMS), Naval Communications, Telecommunications Stations (NCTS's) and Maritime Operations Centers (MOC's).

 [P40A / NCIS Data Modernization & Analytical Tools]: The Naval Criminal Investigative Service (NCIS) mission is to support the day-to-day operations to Prevent Terrorism, Protect National Secrets, Reduce
 Crime across the Department of the Navy (DON) and comply with DON Mandates. NCIS IT Programs and Technical Support are essential to fulfill its mission.

 [P40A / Electronic Military Personnel Record System (EMPRS)]: Electronic Military Personnel Record System (EMPRS) is an electronic document/image based system that serves as the official repository for
 all Department of Navy (DON) official military personnel record images (over 170 million images). It supports active, reserve and retired/separated military personnel in the functional areas of selection board
 operations, casualty management, mobilization, veteran benefits (providing automated Sailor information to the Veterans Administration) and other military personnel management functions. EMPRS annually
 supports over 150 statutory and administrative selection boards, providing over 12 million service record images, covering promotions, assignments, and retention.

 [P40A / Workstations (Sel Board)]: Funding supports retired, active, and reserve military personnel in the functional areas of selection board operations, casualty management, mobilization, veteran benefits
 (providing automated Sailor information to the Veterans Administration) and other military personnel management functions.

 [P40A / Peripherals]: Funds support peripherals, which are auxiliary and infrastructure components, that make up and are associated with the EMPRS system.

BSA 2: Command Support Equipment
ID Code (A=Service Ready, B=Not Service Ready): A                      Program Elements for Code B Items: N/A                                  Other Related Program Elements: N/A
Line Item MDAP/MAIS Code: N/A
 [P40A / Data Center Consolidation (DCC)]: Funds provided for Data Center and Application Optimization, provide sustainment of data center hardware, software, and licenses for established Component
 Enterprise Data Centers (CEDCs) and other approved hosting locations as part of the Information Technology (IT) efficiency for the Manpower, Training, and Education (MPT&E) Data Center footprint. The Navy
 Data Center and commercial cloud resourcing Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) will be implemented by MPT&E along with application hosting costs, continues with consolidation and application migration
 efforts, and ensure that systems are modernized to sustain security requirements of Navy information and data residing within applications and data centers.

[P40A / Cyber Protection and Response Capability (CPRC)]: Funding supports the increases of capabilities to protect Facilities Related Control Systems (FRCS). The Cyber Protection and Response Capability
(CPRC) will develop response capabilities based on collected intelligence, will function as the rapid response team when cyber incidents occur, synchronize FRCS cyber defensive measures, and postures
deployed infrastructure to maintain supply chain lethality.

[P40A / More Situational Awareness - Industrial Control Systems (MOSAICS)]: Funding provided for capabilities to improve situational awareness for cyber defender response, disrupt adversary kill chain in an
acceptable response threshold, and limit adversary reuse of advanced persistent attacks through enhanced sharing of indicators and mitigations.

[P40A / P-130 NAS Sigonella - Comms Center (79S)]: Funds provided for Command, Control, Communications, Computers, Collaboration, and Intelligence (C5I) at communications center NAS Sigonella.
The new P-130 facility will provide increased mission efficiency and the flexibility to accommodate future missions. Equipment requirements include Defense Information Systems Agency (DISA) Transport,
Command, Control, Communications, Computers, and Intelligence (C4I) Distribution Cabling, Audio/Visual Capability, and High Frequency Global Communications System (HFGCS) Scope Command.

[P40A / P-131 NCTAMS Det Souda Bay - Comms Center (79S)]: Funds provided for Command, Control, Communications, Computers, and Intelligence/Information Technology (C4I/IT) equipment. Due to an
increase in network and communication responsibilities as the missions of the existing tenant commands on the base slowly increase, updated equipment is required.

[P40A / BCO Infrastructure Replacement (Cable & Equipment) (40B)]: Funding is provided to support of Base Communications Office (BCO) Infrastructure Replacement at Naval Computer and
Telecommunications Station Far East (NCTSFE). Components of the NCTSFE are replaced on cyclical basis and recurring to ensure optimal functionality of the BCO.

[P40A / High Frequency Global Communication System (HFGCS 36A1)]: HFGCS procurement replaces antennas at various locations. HFGCS is the primary High Frequency (HF) platform directly supporting
U.S. Navy, President of the United States (POTUS), and other Government agencies (air-ground-air/ship-to-shore).

[P40A / Live Virtual Constructive (LVC)Training (61K)]: Funding is provided to support a realistic IW virtual capability into Live, Virtual, and Constructive (LVC) events via Navy Continuous Training Environment
(NCTE). Supports holistic readiness through force generation of effectively trained, resilient Information Warfare forces.

[P40A / NAVCENT Rapid Response Kits]: Funds support Joint Maritime Ashore Support Team (JMAST) required to support ashore operations of deployed maritime commanders as a mobile Task Force
Command Center (TFCC) and to provide the Joint Force Maritime Component Commander an expeditionary scalable command and control capability ashore for Continuity of Operations (COOP). JMAST Rapid
Response Kits (RRKs) to support the Israel Crisis and associated contingency operations

[P40A / NAVCENT - JMAST Early Entry Kits (EEK)]: Funds support Joint Maritime Ashore Support Team (JMAST) to support ashore operations of deployed maritime commanders as a mobile Task Force
Command Center (TFCC) and to provide the Joint Force Maritime Component Commander an expeditionary scalable command and control capability ashore.

[P40A / NAVCENT - VTC Upgrade/ MS TEAMS Integration]: Funds support NAVCENT VTC/ MS Teams management and communication with the many geographically separated NAVCENT teams and partners.
This capability enhances collaboration and reduces the need for travel costs.

[P40A / European Deterrence Initiative]: Funding is to provide physical security measures at multiple locations in the Naval Forces Europe - Africa AOR to minimize West African campaign challenges for U.S.
Forces in Somalia. Funds for physical security measures within the Naval Forces Europe - Africa AOR request for OMN vice OPN funding.

Justification:
The FY 2026 request was reduced by $0.239 million for Advisory and Assistance Services to promote efficiencies and advance the policies of the Administration in alignment with Executive Order 14222,
"Implementing the President's Department of Government Efficiency Cost Efficiency Initiative."

The FY 2026 request was reduced by $0.047 million for civilian personnel to optimize the workforce in compliance with Executive Order 14210, "Implementing the President's Department of Government
Efficiency Workforce Optimization Initiative."

1.1 Naval Criminal Investigative Service (NCIS) Data Modernization & Analytical Tools: FY26 increase ($0.083M) supports equipment refresh of components of the Law Enforcement & Investigation (LE/INV)
Case Management System, tactical network and VTC infrastructure initiatives.

5.3 Network Drives: FY26 increase ($0.097M) is due to sustainment of the EMPRS program, providing technology refreshment (TR) throughout the years (evolutionary) as opposed to a TR every eight years.

5.4 Storage Devices: FY26 increase ($0.110M) is due to reduced quantity of storage devices for Network Devices in association with Electronic Military Personnel Records System (EMPRS) required. Funds
provided for life cycle replacement of storage devices.

5.5 Workstations (Sel Board): FY26 decrease ($0.173M) is due to reduced requirement for additional workstations in association with workstations for the Selection Board with Electronic Military Personnel
Records System (EMPRS).

5.6 Scanners High Speed: FY26 increase ($0.236M) is provided for High-Speed Scanners in association with EMPRS. Electronic Military Personnel Records System (EMPRS) is an electronic document/image-
based system that serves as the repository for all Department of the Navy official military personnel record images. Funds provide for life cycle replacement of network devices.

5.7 Peripherals: FY26 decrease ($0.122M) is due to lower peripherals required. Peripherals include items such as removable walls within boardrooms that function as display screen for military personnel record
display, keyboards, printers, monitors, mice for workstations in the boardrooms, etc. Peripherals represent auxiliary and infrastructure components that make up and are associated with the EMPRS system.
EMPRS is the major information system which is mutually supported by infrastructure to conduct 160 selection boards on an annual basis.

5.11 Records Mgmt. Software: FY26 increase ($0.288M) provided for procurement of software in support of the Electronic Military Personnel Records System (EMPRS). Electronic Military Personnel Records
System (EMPRS) is an electronic document/image-based system that serves as the repository for all Department of the Navy official military personnel record images. Funds provide for life cycle replacement of
network devices.

5.13 Data Center Consolidation (DCC): FY26 increase ($0.436M) supports the consolidation and application migration efforts and ensure that systems are modernized to sustain security requirements. Funding
supports the consolidation and application migration efforts and systems modernization. Funds provided sustainment of data center hardware, software, and licenses for established Component Enterprise Data
Centers (CEDCs) and other approved hosting locations.

ID Code (A=Service Ready, B=Not Service Ready): A                     Program Elements for Code B Items: N/A                                 Other Related Program Elements: N/A
Line Item MDAP/MAIS Code: N/A

9.1. PDAS Equipment Modernization: Planning and Decision Aid System (PDAS) is a classified, protected program operated by the Navy. PDAS is a command-and-control system that provides capabilities to
Office of the Secretary of Defense, services, Unified Combatant Commanders (CCMD), Intelligence Community and Coalition partners for operational planning/coordination of special access programs (SAPs).
PDAS enables Integrated Joint Special Technical operations (IJSTO) to incorporate SAPs into operational planning and execution from battlefield operators through the Commander-in-Chief in support of national
security objectives. PDAS is the system for the Chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff (CJCS) for IJSTO.

11.1 - 14.1 (NAVWAR) Maritime Operations Center (MOC): FY26 decrease ($0.092M) supports MOC Workstations (MWS) system procurements, production engineering, integration and installations required to
continue incremental improvements of the common core infrastructure and Command, Control, Communications, Computers and Intelligence (C4I) capabilities of the MOCs. This leads to fully integrated, globally
networked operational-level commands with a Navy enterprise network and appropriate capabilities to exercise Navy missions Command and Control (C2), such as Ballistic Missile Defense (BMD), in accordance
with the MOC Core Baseline/ Mission Build (CB/MB) baseline requirements.

15.1 Facilities Related Control Systems (FRCS): FY26 decrease ($3.437M) due to required components having been procured in previous FY and moving into an OMN supported phase of the mission to secure
the critical sites.

15.4 More Situational Awareness Industrial Control Systems (MOSAICS): FY26 increase ($3.822M) is due to needed framework components i.e. hardware/software procurements as well as continued system
development in order to fully build out and implement MOSAICS across the enterprise as MOSAICS is our solution to deliver defensive cyber capabilities where none exist to implement needed security controls.

16.9 High Frequency Global Communication System (HFGCS 36A1): FY26 funding supports the procurement of antennas for Yokota and Guam. The High Frequency Global Communications System (HFGCS)
is a network of single sideband shortwave transmitters used to communicate with aircraft in flight, ground stations, and Navy surface assets.

16.10 Live Virtual Constructive (LVC) Training: FY26 increase ($7.207M) to fund development and integration of Live, Virtual, Constructive (LVC) enabled training events.

      FY26 increase ($0.083M) supports equipment refresh of components of the Law Enforcement & Investigation (LE/INV) Case Management System, tactical network and VTC infrastructure initiatives.
(2)
      FY26 increase due to the type of tech refresh required for the workstations.
(3)

(4)

(5)

(7)

(MWS) systems.
(9)
      FY26 increase due to different equipment sets required for the MOC C2 systems.
(10)
    FY26 decrease in DSA stems from operational efficiencies gained during previous tasking, enabling the program to prioritize additional procurement, integration and installation in order to more rapidly deploy latest C4I technologies to
higher HQs, joint peers, mission partners, partner nations, and maritime tactical forces.
(11)
       FY26 increase ($0.040K) in funding is to support P-130 commencement of C4I integration planning efforts.

(13)
       FY26 decrease is related to funding move to new cost element 16.9.
(14)

(15)
   FY26 increase ($4.569K) will be used for development and integration of IW-informed LVC-enabled training events for Fleet Response Training Plan (FRTP) and deliver one fully functional IW Team Training Facilities (IWTTF) in Norfolk,
and one minimum viable IWTTF in San Diego for unit, staff, and fleet-level training, TTP (tactics, techniques and procedures) development, exercises, and mission rehearsal.

 Description:
 The Medical Support Equipment (MSE) program funds new medical capability and new technology on naval operating ships. MSE configuration management, spares, technical manuals and installations are also
 funded through this line item. Requirements are determined through Commander, U.S. Fleet Forces Command (USFFC) and Commander, U.S. Pacific Fleet (PACFLT), and procurement is managed by Program
 Executive Office Unmanned and Small Combatants (PEO USC), Program Management Support Expeditionary Missions (PMS 408). Funding is used to procure modifications or additions to the ships' allowance
 items that are identified within the Authorized Medical Allowance List (AMAL) and Authorized Dental Allowance List (ADAL). AMALs and ADALs are unique to specific classes or types of ship or Commands
 to fulfill its intended health care mission. AMAL and ADAL allowances are approved by the respective Fleet Type Commander (TYCOM) Force Surgeon with concurrence by the Commander, Fleet Forces
 Command Surgeon. The initiation of a change to the current depth (allowance amount) of material or addition of a new allowance for an item(s) within an AMAL or ADAL results from either a change in the scope
 of care a specific ship must provide in accordance with its Required Operational Capability (ROC) and Projected Operational Environment (POE) and/or a change in the standard of medical care the assigned
 personnel are expected to provide. Changes to AMAL and ADAL requirements are determined through Individual Allowance Change Requests, as a result of reviews chaired by TYCOM Fleet Surgeons.

 The Fleet Hospital/Expeditionary Medical Facility (EMF) program funds critical medical equipment to support and provide theater hospitalization capabilities (commonly referred to as Role III Care) as defined in
 Joint Publication 4-02 Health Services Support and the EMF ROC and POE. This funding is used to procure investment equipment that supports the standard of care for Casualty Management as determined by
 the DoD Combat Trauma Surgery Committee.

 Funds in this line item will be used to procure medical equipment such as Direct Digital Radiology System (DDRS) and Computer Aided Design and Computer Aided Manufacturing (CAD CAM) Milling Machine,
 Expeditionary Medical Unit (EMU), Expeditionary Resuscitative Surgical Systems (ERSS), En Route Care Systems (ERCS), and Certified Diagnostic Mobile Lab (CDML). Funding provides support the Hospital
 Ships (USNS COMFORT (USFFC) and USNS MERCY (PACFLT)) major systems replacement. The program is part of the standardization and life cycle management of the MERCY Class Hospital ships
 sustainability plan.

 [P40A / Direct Digital Radiology - Procurement]: Funds provided for Direct Digital Radiology systems required for advanced diagnostic imaging. This provides shipboard medical personnel the capability to
 digitally process, and store images thereby reducing film development time, maintenance, and storage costs. It also provides seamless integration of digital imaging with other systems used in Telemedicine,
 creating a more efficient process that bypasses chemical processing and provides the ability to digitally transfer and enhance images.

 [P40A / Direct Digital Radiology - Installation]: Funds provided for Direct Digital Radiology system installation. The systems are required for advanced diagnostic imaging.



LI 8109 - Medical Support Equipment                                                                     UNCLASSIFIED
                                                                                                                                                                                                    Volume 5 - 191
Navy                                                                                                            Page 1 of 7                                  P-1 Line #158

Exhibit P-40, Budget Line Item Justification: PB 2026 Navy                                                                                                      Date: June 2025
Appropriation / Budget Activity / Budget Sub Activity:                                                    P-1 Line Item Number / Title:
1810N: Other Procurement, Navy / BA 07: Personnel & Command Support Equip /                               8109 / Medical Support Equipment
BSA 2: Command Support Equipment
ID Code (A=Service Ready, B=Not Service Ready): A                      Program Elements for Code B Items: N/A                                 Other Related Program Elements: N/A
Line Item MDAP/MAIS Code: N/A
 [P40A / Radiography/Fluoroscopy, Digital]: Funding provided for updated Radiography/Fluoroscopy (RAD) for USNS MERCY. These units are needed to perform specialized procedures supporting both
 humanitarian and combat missions.

 [P40A / Expeditionary Resuscitative Surgical Systems (ERSS)]: Expeditionary Resuscitative Surgical Systems (ERSSs) are the smallest standard force package of the Navy Expeditionary Health Services
 Support (NEHSS) family and may be employed afloat, on any vessel, or ashore to provide immediate life and/or limb saving damage control resuscitation and surgery. It is designed to support disaggregated
 maritime operations and operate in austere environments.

 [P40A / En Route Care Systems (ERCS)]: En Route Care Systems (ERCSs) are the core capability to patient movement. ERCSs ensure patient degradation does not occur during transport to the next level of
 care.

 [P40A / Certified Diagnostic Mobile Lab (CDML)]: Certified Diagnostic Mobile Lab (CDML) provide a modern, agile, and scalable capability to conduct in-theater diagnostics and screening of infectious diseases,
 both ashore and afloat. CDMLs provide the capability to diagnose infectious diseases in an expeditionary environment. CDMLs are on ships to assess infection and mitigate outbreak.

 [P40A / Production Engineering Support]: Funding supports activities used to identify, plan, resource, and acquire facilities, hardware, software, documentation, manpower and personnel necessary for planning
 and management of mission critical Expeditionary Medical Family of Systems components.

 Justification:
 The FY 2026 request was reduced by $0.086 million for Advisory and Assistance Services to promote efficiencies and advance the policies of the Administration in alignment with Executive Order 14222,
 "Implementing the President's Department of Government Efficiency Cost Efficiency Initiative."

 The FY 2026 request was reduced by $0.023 million for civilian personnel to optimize the workforce in compliance with Executive Order 14210, "Implementing the President's Department of Government
 Efficiency Workforce Optimization Initiative."

 FY 2026 BUMED (BSO 18) decrease ($0.347M) supports requirements for laboratory, X-Ray, Biomedical, preventative medicine, physical therapy, operating room, intensive care, acute care, and dental
 equipment.

 FY 2026 NAVSEA (BSO 24) Deployable Medical System increase ($2.464M) includes (+$3.400M) increase for EXMED SMART Warehousing and (-$0.936) decrease for rate and other budgetary adjustments.

 FY 2026 PACFLT (BSO 70) increase ($0.021M) supports the overhaul and upgrade of medical equipment required for the USNS MERCY.

 Description:
 Fleet SCI IT: This effort delivers and sustains Sensitive Compartmented Information (SCI) network access and information technology (IT) infrastructure and tools to the Maritime Operations Centers and Naval
 Intelligence Community ashore. This includes desktop hardware, software, and peripherals, network equipment, server hardware and software, video teleconferencing devices, and secure voice over Inernet
 Protocol (IP) telephones.

 Office of Naval Intelligence (ONI) Charade RED: (CR) Program terminated, per PBIS Issue 54600. Details available at a higher classification and are submitted in the Congressional Justification Books.

 [P40A / Fleet Intelligence Capital Equipment Replacement Program (CERP)]: Maintain and modernize Navy GEOINT Tasking, Collection, Processing, Exploitation, and Dissemination (TCPED) distributed
 operations node. Additionally, maintain and modernize modification (MOD) and CERP systems to ensure interoperability with Fleet, Joint, and NGA PED infrastructure and ISR platforms. Space MOD will
 increase processing, exploitation, and Dissemination (PED) capacity to support 10 lines of Aircraft Intelligence, Surveillance, and reconnaissance (AISR) data. MOD Space is re-engineering 17,345 SQFT of
 analyst's spaces that includes new fiber, critical power, additional routers for the GEOINT Sub-network, build of network closets, additional power drops, and additional desks to support AISR GEOINT PED and
 Multi-INT NRT operations ISO of the Fleet.

 [P40A / SCI Network CERP for MOCs]: Provide resources for a critical, must-fund change proposal (CP) to satisfy COMPACFLT requirement to provision Joint Worldwide Intelligence Communications System
 (JWICS) in Maritime Operation Centers (MOCs) to provide Fleet Commanders' intelligence on Russia, Ukraine, North Korea and other countries. Project will improve SCI coalition network enclaves on force level
 ships and the MOCs.

 [P40A / SCI Communications at MOCs]: SCI services at the MOC transfer to Fleet SCI IT for communication services. Fleet SCI IT (Hopper ISC) will be the single service provider for MOCs ashore, supporting
 messaging, compartmented, AOR unique and intelligence services.

 [P40A / SCI Information Assurance (IA)]: Funding enables Navy tactical SCI program capabilities needed for the establishment of an initial capability and fully operational capability to meet full compliance with
 ICD 502 and provide active cyber defense and incident response of the information environment for the IC and establishes the framework for risk management and authorization (RMF) across all systems that
 process, store and/or transmit SCI

 Justification:
 Fleet SCI IT: Funding enables continued network and storage equipment sustainment and modernization for the Naval Intelligence community. Funding enables continued network and storage equipment
 sustainment and modernizes SCI coalition network enclaves on force level ships and the Maritime Operations Centers (MOCs).

 ONI RED (CR): Program terminated, per PBIS Issue 54600. Funding is used to finance long-term investments for the procurement, production and modernization of satellite communications and electronics
 equipment required for the sensitive sensor tracking capabilities hosted on LYNX (G5) Mobile User Objective System (MUOS) Ground Station Infrastructure platform systems. The program must acquire and
 configure advanced LYNX (G5) MUOS satellite communications processing system and sensors components to meet advanced capabilities in support of maritime tracking requirements for US Navy platforms,
 unmanned systems and Intelligence Community directorates, in for every Combatant Commands (COCOM).

Exhibit P-40, Budget Line Item Justification: PB 2026 Navy                                                                                                                    Date: June 2025

 Description:
 This budget line item funds procurement of various items of equipment to support the operating forces at Naval Stations and related facilities, including, but not limited to the following:

 Live Firefighting Trainer: Funds provided for new construction, shipping, set-up, and operator training for live fire mobile training devices. The specific live fire device shall be configured to provide both structural
 fire scenarios, various shipboard fire scenarios and confined space rescue scenarios during hands-on training. Commander, Navy Installations Command (CNIC) Fire and Emergency Services (F&ES) staffing
 will increase during the next 5 years to meet the new requirements of shipboard manning as a result of the USS Bonhomme Richard (BHR) recommendations requiring the firefighters to receive the required
 training for certification and proficiency skills in the new trainers located at installations selected. Live fire trainers are essential to supporting Navy live fire training requirements for civilian and military response
 crews. Inflation costs are factored in to determine amount and type of trainers to be purchased.

 Mobile Aircraft Training Devices: Funds provided for new construction, shipping, set-up, and operator training for various types of mobile aircraft fire training devices. The live fire device shall have the ability to
 safely produce propane fuel fires for specific types of aircrafts such as large frame, fighter and helicopters during hands-on training per the selected vendor, supporting Navy live fire training requirements for
 civilian and military response crews.

 Shipboard Firefighting Responders, Equipment, and JRM Services: Program provides all-hazards response to shore locations including specialized response to Navy ports. Fund Shipboard Firefighting
 Responders, Specialty Equipment, and Suppression Apparatus, and Increase Joint Region Marianas (JRM) Fire and Emergency Services to meet mission requirements. Increased funding implements corrective
 action resulting from Board USS Bonhomme Richard Learning 2 Action and Major Fires review. Recent losses to fleet assets have shown a severe deficiency in the ability to supply large capacity water and
 personnel resources to battle major shipboard fires while docked at Navy piers. Professional CNIC F&ES firefighters do not have the proper tools to gain access and provide uninterrupted agent application in
 hazardous conditions, which directly increase the risks of more significant property loss due to rapid fire spread. Request spans increases to current equipment levels (eg hose adapters, nozzles and SCBA
 compressors), new types of equipment (e.g. firehose that also deliver SCBA air to firefighters) and technology (eg, tablets, thermal imagers and unmanned agent application). Equipment will be allocated to
 locations with shipboard firefighting requirement. Requested capabilities and funds are not currently in baseline funding. JRM services identified risk to submarines, tenders, Port Operations and ship repair
 facilities from current manning deficit increases mission degradation. Additional personnel and the requirement of building new fire station for Polaris Point is immediate priority to be completed.

 Fender/Bumper/Separator Systems: Procurement includes various size and shaped energy absorbing cushions placed between a pier and a ship/submarine or between two ships/submarines. Multiple fenders
 may be used with different size and types of ships. Various types of filling of air or other material may be procured. Standard type is hydro-pneumatic rubber fender made IAW ISO 17357. May be used for both
 submarine and surface ships.


LI 8118 - Operating Forces Support Equipment                                                            UNCLASSIFIED
                                                                                                                                                                                                              Volume 5 - 209
Navy                                                                                                            Page 1 of 6                                  P-1 Line #161
                                                                                                  UNCLASSIFIED
Exhibit P-40, Budget Line Item Justification: PB 2026 Navy                                                                                                              Date: June 2025
Appropriation / Budget Activity / Budget Sub Activity:                                                          P-1 Line Item Number / Title:
1810N: Other Procurement, Navy / BA 07: Personnel & Command Support Equip /                                     8118 / Operating Forces Support Equipment
BSA 2: Command Support Equipment
ID Code (A=Service Ready, B=Not Service Ready): A                         Program Elements for Code B Items: N/A                                     Other Related Program Elements: N/A
Line Item MDAP/MAIS Code: N/A

 Composite CVN Camels: Procurement of large floating metal or composite structures designed to maintain the proper distance for Carrier Vessel/Nuclear Carrier Vessel (CV/CVNs) to keep the ships from
 being damaged or damaging the pier structure. Composite CVN Camels are used against the pier to moor an Aircraft carrier on a pier. The camels go against the pier (between the pier and ship) to protect the
 infrastructure/pier and the ship from damage. It also puts distance between the ship and pier. When USN Carriers are placed alongside a pier the hull design dictates the need to use standoff equipment. The
 camels requested provide the necessary standoff. These composite camels offer considerable reduction in maintenance due to constructed material. As currently utilized ship support equipment ages it becomes
 necessary to replace that equipment. Equipment procurement budgets are not adequate to keep up with the demand for ship support equipment. As more ships are placed into service the requirement exceeds
 currently available support equipment.

 Submarine Deep Draft Camels: Procurement of very large floating metal structures designed to maintain the proper distance for submarines to keep them from being damaged by the pier. These may be for
 specific submarine classes (e.g. Fast Attack), or universal (i.e. for all submarine classes). They are used against the pier to moor a submarine on a pier. The camels go against the pier (between the pier and
 submarine) to protect the infrastructure/pier and the submarine from damage. It puts distance between the submarine to protect special sensors from damage.

 Universal Submarine Composite Camels: Procurement of universal pierside, composite camels used to breast submarines from piers/wharves. They are fabricated of universal composite material with a
 modular design. Size is approximately 36 ft. W x 18 ft. H x 17.6 ft. D. Suitable for all classes of submarines.

 Paint Floats: procurement of floating assets that are used by Port Operations and Ship's Force personnel to facilitate external ship work (blasting, painting, and other maintenance). They can also be used as
 separators or camels. These can be towed into place, or may even be self-propelled. They may have permanent or movable work platforms installed as needed to assist in reaching work sites. Some may have
 self-ballasting mechanisms to help maintain stability and come in various sizes.

 Composite Sub camels: The composite Submarine camels significantly reduce the amount of maintenance required to keep the units serviceable. Older models, reaching the end of serviceability are made of
 steel and in some cases wood. To overhaul these units requires considerable manpower. The older units do not provide the same level of hull protection the composite units provide. Equipment procurement
 budgets are not adequate to keep up with the demand for ship support. As more ships are placed into service the requirement exceeds currently available support equipment.

 DDG Separator/Ships separators: Are designed to allow ships to breast outboard another ship in support of heavy port loading. Ships separators are used to moor two ships together/alongside of each other.
 When there's many ships in port and pier space is limited, some installations have to place ships (DDG CLASS) together.

 Utility Floats: Used to moor to an existing pier to moor/dock small craft and tugboats, the current pier configurations does not allow for a safe and effective moor of small craft and tugs.

 Hydro Pneumatic Sub Fender; the procurement of large floating Pneumatic fenders are designed to maintain the proper standoff distance between submarines when nested and keeping them from being
 damaged. They also protect the submarine and pier structure when used pier side. These fenders are designed to last 10 years. The current inventory of Hydro-pneumatic fenders are past their service life and
 require replacement.

 Logistics Data System (LDS) camels: The LDS Surface Ship Camel was designed to berth DDG and CG class surface vessels. The pier designs require an LDS type camel while berthed. The updated design
 supports various fleet assets which are designed to maintain the proper standoff distance between nested vessels and keeping them from being damaged.

 Collateral Equipment: Funds provided for equipment and minor property required to initially outfit new construction financed by the, Military Construction, Navy appropriation or Host Nation funds as outlined in
 OPNAVINST 11010.20 series (Facilities Projects Manual).

 [P40A / Live FirefightingTrainer Procurement]: Funds provided for construction, shipping, set-up, and operator training for a live fire mobile training devices. The live fire device shall have the ability to safely
 produce propane fuel fires during hands-on training per the selected vendor, supporting Navy live fire training requirements for civilian and military response crews.
