 [P40A / Mobile Aircraft Training Devices]: Funds provided for construction, shipping, set-up, and operator training for a mobile aircraft fire training devices. The live fire device shall have the ability to safely
 produce propane fuel fires during hands-on training per the selected vendor, supporting Navy live fire training requirements for civilian and military response crews.

 [P40A / Shipboard Firefighting Responders, Equipment, JRM Services]: Funds provided for shipboard firefighting responders, specialty and equipment, and suppression apparatus, and increase Joint Region
 Marianas (JRM) Fire and Emergency Services to meet mission requirements.

 [P40A / Port Operations Waterfront Support Equipment]: Funds are provided for the procurement of various waterfront equipment in support of fleet requirements during in port periods. This includes paint floats
 for hull maintenance, brows and platforms for egress, Big Deck ships ramps for onloading deployment assets, floats for docking tugs, utility floats to support submarines and pontoons for docking small boats.

 [P40A / Port Operations Fendering Systems]: Funds provided for the procurement of Surface Ship Camels, Fenders, and Ships Separators, designed to berth Surface Ships and Submarines to CNIC installations
 piers/wharfs.

 [P40A / Collateral Equipment]: Collateral Equipment: Funds provided for equipment and minor property required to initially outfit new construction financed by the, Military Construction, Navy appropriation or Host
 Nation funds as outlined in OPNAVINST 11010.20 series (Facilities Projects Manual).

Flyaway Unit Cost ($ in Thousands)                            -              -               -              -              -               -              -               -         -            -     -            -
Gross/Weapon System Unit Cost ($ in Thousands)                -              -               -              -              -               -              -               -         -            -     -            -


 Description:
 Navy Expeditionary Command, Control, Communications, Computers, & Intelligence (C4I): C2 Modernization efforts include the procurement, assembly, integration, production testing, and initial training of Small,
 Medium and Large Scale Communication Systems, and the C4I Tactical Radio Modernization & Communications across Navy Expeditionary Forces (NEF), both Active Component (AC) and Reserve Component
 (RC). These efforts also include convergence to a common Shore-Based Expeditionary C4I baseline, a single cybersecurity Risk Management Framework (RMF) Authority to Operate (ATO), and the outfitting of
 Expeditionary Forces' Table of Allowances (TOA) equipment requirements, which includes Wideband Anti Jam Modem System (WAMS) in support of the Expeditionary Deployable Node (EDN), Navy Enterprise
 Tactical Command and Control (NETC2), Rapid Response Kits (RRK), Expeditionary Tactical Assault Kit (ETAK), and Secure Enclave Access Kits (SEAKIT) (CENTRIX-S Fly Away Kit), which supports the
 complex communication and coordination needs of expeditionary forces.

 [P5 / Navy Expeditionary C4I - C2 Modernization - Active Component]: Funds provided for procuring Expeditionary Deployable Node (EDN) for Active and Reserve Components, Rapid Response Kits (RRK), and
 Expeditionary Tactical Assault Kits (ETAK) as approved in the Table of Allowance (TOA) equipment requirements within Expeditionary Forces.

 [P5 / Navy Expeditionary C4I - C2 Modernization - Reserve Component]: The program is procuring EDNs for Active and Reserve Components, RRKs, ETAK. and SEAKIT CENTRIX-S FAK, as approved in the
 TOA equipment requirements within Expeditionary Forces.

 [P5 / Navy Expeditionary C4I - Tactical Radio Modernization AN/PRC-163]: Funding supports the procurement of AN/PRC-163 Controlled Cryptographic Items (CCI) Multi-Channel Handheld Radio which are
 required to extend the reach of real-time intelligence from command to squad level with seamless crossbanding of satellite communications (SATCOM), Line-of-Sight (LOS) and Beyond Line-of-Sight (BLOS)
 networks. Access encrypted data from a wide variety of platforms and sensors, while simultaneously networking. Even geographically isolated units can share voice and data, while advancing situational
 awareness (SA) with an ISR mission module that adds full motion video (FMV) capabilities, as approved in the TOA equipment requirements within Expeditionary Forces.

 [P5 / Navy Expeditionary C4I - Tactical Radio Modernization AN/PRC-167]: Funding supports the procurement of AN/PRC 167 Wideband and MUOS-ready radios which are required to meet the multi-domain
 challenges of any combination of ground, vehicular and airborne missions, simultaneously and independently connecting warfighters through the full frequency range of a broad portfolio of waveforms on each of
 two channels, as approved in the TOA equipment requirements within Expeditionary Forces.

ID Code (A=Service Ready, B=Not Service Ready): A                   Program Elements for Code B Items: N/A                               Other Related Program Elements: N/A
Line Item MDAP/MAIS Code: N/A
 [P5 / Navy Expeditionary C4I - Tactical Radio Modernization AN/PRC-171]: Funding supports the procurement of AN/PRC 171 Non-Controlled Cryptographic Items (CCI) ensures connectivity with ground-based
 tactical teams by supporting multiple modes of operation, including wideband MANET and narrowband voice and PLI. Secure, reliable communications in contested and congested environments are supported
 with in-field upgrades to emerging wideband and narrowband resilient waveforms.

 [P5 / Navy Expeditionary C4I - Wideband Anti Jam Modem System]: Funding supports the Navy's next generation wideband satellite communications modem that will be integrated with the Navy multiband
 satellite terminals for communications over the wideband global satellite communications constellation. WAMS will replace the AC2 modems. WAMS is the Navy component of the Protected Anti-Jam Tactical
 SATCOM (PATS) Joint Program.

 Justification:
 FY26 increase ($3.339M) supports hardware procurements, testing, integration, assembly and fielding for ETAK Command and Control (C2) Modernization and Tactical Radio Modernization in support of
 the Portable Radio Tactical Program. Funding increase is required for procurement, integration, fielding and modernization of tactical C4I small, medium and large Scale Communication Systems for Naval
 Expeditionary forces. These systems include Expeditionary Deployable Node (EDN), Rapid Response Kits (RRK), Expeditionary Tactical Assault Kit (ETAK), and Secure Enclave Access Kits (SEAKIT)
 (CENTRIX-S Fly Away Kit), which supports the complex communication and coordination needs of expeditionary forces and starting in FY26 the Wideband Anti Jam Modem System (WAMS).

 engagement status. FY26 average unit costs updated to reflect costs associated with latest configuration which includes cost for additional ancillary equipment such as cable assembly and mission function kits

 Description:
 DIGITAL SIDE SCAN SONAR (SHIP): Additional high-speed, high-resolution side scan sonar systems are required to meet requirements supporting Mine Warfare (MIW) Operations. The intended system
 procured will be installed aboard USNS HENSON and additional T-AGS 60 class ships to replicate the system aboard USNS HEEZEN. The procurement will facilitate simultaneous collection of high-resolution
 imagery at mine warfare resolutions and frequencies. The imagery data is required to generate products that directly support mine warfare, hydrographic, and oceanographic requirements. Side scan sonar data
 is critical for the detection of small mine-like targets as well as hazards-to-navigation (e.g. wrecks) and characterizing the sea floor over large areas (geoprovincing). This data is fundamental to execute MIW
 change-detection doctrine comparing historical data to current data to determine mine-threats.

 LONG TERM AMBIENT NOISE RECORDING AND REPORTING SYSTEM: This is a moored, acoustic buoy system used to support ISR missions. The buoys are four channel Environmental Acoustic Recording
 System (EARS) units that will record ambient noise for long time periods within an 8kHz bandwidth. The Environmental Acoustic Recording System buoys will have to be recovered for data processing.

 SHIP MOVING VESSEL PROFILER (MVP): MVP systems are employed to measure sound velocity from a moving platform, providing practical high-accuracy measurements while the vessel remains underway
 in survey status. These measurements are critical to meeting accuracy requirements for hydrographic and bathymetric surveys. Shipboard MVP (MVP-300) is the larger complement to the Hydrographic Survey
 Launch (HSL) MVP (MVP-30) and is intended for use aboard T-AGS 60 class platforms exclusively. System consists of a multi-sensor towbody, computer-controlled winch, over-the-side handling system, and
 control/data logging workstation. It permits rapid and automated acquisition of Sound Velocity Profile (SVP) data from an underway vessel, significantly increasing multibeam survey accuracy, particularly in
 complex water masses.

 SHIP SUBMERSIBLE BALLISTIC NUCLEAR SUPPORT NAVO (85M): Funding is for the procurement of non-acoustic oceanographic measurement systems in support of requirements to assess vulnerability
 threats to Ship Submersible Ballistic, Nuclear (SSBN) against emergent adversary technologies. Advances in technology provide the means for enhanced environmental characterization and these purchases will
 enable persistent non-acoustic sensing/data collection, and product generation to characterize oceanographic regions of interest.

 HYDROGRAPHIC SURVEY LAUNCH (HSL), UNMANNED SURFACE VESSELS (USV): Hull Replacement - Funding to procure USVs with Launch and Recovery System (LARS) to support hydrographic data
 collection requirements. This will provide NAVOCEANO the capability of providing the HSL-outfitted T-AGS 60 ships with a complimentary pair of USVs that will add an open ocean, blue water USV capability.

1810N: Other Procurement, Navy / BA 07: Personnel & Command Support Equip /                                 8126 / Environmental Support Equipment
BSA 2: Command Support Equipment
ID Code (A=Service Ready, B=Not Service Ready): A                       Program Elements for Code B Items: N/A                                   Other Related Program Elements: N/A
Line Item MDAP/MAIS Code: N/A
 SHIP TO SHORE DATA COMS: The C-BAND system provides two-way satellite voice and data broadband communications while underway on an ocean-going vessel. This system is the primary vehicle to
 transfer data from the ship to the office. C-BAND is critical for providing remote Information Awareness/Information Technology (IA/IT) configuration and upgrade support required for the T-AGS to maintain
 Authorization to Operate (ATO) approval.

 CONDUCTIVITY TEMPERATURE DEPTH (CTD) OPTICS PINGER SYSTEM: The Environmental CTD-OPTICS-Pinger Systems are a Life Cycle Replacement (LCR) of the Existing T-AGS 60 Class shipboard
 system, which has a 10 year LCR period. These systems acquire physical oceanographic data in all water depths used to create hydrographic, bathymetric, acoustic and environmental products in support of
 safety of navigation, expeditionary warfare, naval special warfare, anti-submarine warfare and mine warfare. These systems also provide synoptic data, which directly supports ocean modeling requirements for
 the Navy.

 PRIMARY OCEANOGRAPHIC PREDICTION SYS (POPS) & OCEANOGRAPHIC INFORMATION SYSTEM (OIS) (85H): The POPS program delivers High-Performance Computing (HPC) capability and
 information technology (IT) infrastructure on specialized hardware and software systems facilitating acquisition, assimilation, processing, analysis, quality control, storage and delivery of assured meteorology and
 oceanography (METOC) data, information, products and services optimizing weapons and sensors for warfighter support. The Oceanographic Information System (OIS) delivers the operational IT system aboard
 six T-AGS Class Oceanographic Survey ships to meet the DoD and Navy safe navigation and weapon/sensor performance requirements; and generate and disseminate global oceanographic observations and
 forecasts to Naval forces. The program is in operations and sustainment. Lifecycle replacement of End-of-Life/End-of-Service (EoL/EoS) system components on a five-year cycle is required to maintain minimum
 warfighting capability.

 P-001 PRECISE TIME AND ASTROMETRY NETWORK MILCON USNO (84T): Other Procurement Military Construction (OPMLCN) MILCON P-001 (NAVIFOR): The Precise Time and Astrometry Network (PTA
 Network) distributes Precise Time, Earth Orientation Parameters and Celestial Reference Frame data to the Department of Defense (DoD) in support of national strategic priorities. These funds procure Master
 Clock, Earth Orientation Software Correlator and Celestial Reference Frame network assets and provide tech refresh hardware for Master Clock and Correlator.

 OTHER PROCUREMENT PRECISE TIME AND ASTROMETRY (OPPTA) (84T): OPPTA, as directed by Joint Chiefs of Staff Instruction (CJCSI) 6130.01H, supports national strategic priorities by providing
 precise time and astrometry reference frame data to the entire DoD for Assured Position, Navigation and Time (PNT). The Precise Time and Astrometry system is comprised of multiple subsystems including
 assets that generate crucial parameters for GPS. These subsystems undergo lifecycle replacement in accordance with subsystem requirements and break/replace requirements, therefore subsystem
 procurement is tied to the fiscal year and is consistently executed to the budget.

 OTHER PROCUREMENT PRECISE TIME AND ASTROMETRY NETWORK (OPPTAN) (84Z): The Precise Time and Astrometry Network (PTA Network) is the network delivering the United States Naval
 Observatory (USNO) precise time and astrometry reference frame to the entire DoD as required by national strategic priorities. This system consists of ten mission enclave subsystems located across three
 sites in Washington, D.C., Colorado Springs, CO, and Flagstaff, AZ. Lifecycle replacements of mission network architecture are in accordance with enclave requirements, is therefore tied to the fiscal year and is
 consistently executed to budget.

 OTHER PROCUREMENT VERY LONG BASELINE ARRAY RESILIENCE (OPVLBA) (84T): The Very Long Baseline Array (VLBA) is a critical telescope system supporting United States Naval Observatory
 (USNO) Earth Orientation Parameter data acquisition and dissemination as required by Joint Chiefs of Staff Instruction (CJCSI) 6130.01H and national strategic priorities. Very Long Baseline Array Resilience
 captures lifecycle requirements for ten antenna located at ten locations across the United States. These subsystems undergo lifecycle replacement in accordance with subsystem requirements and break/replace
 requirements, therefore subsystem procurement is tied to the fiscal year and is consistently executed to the budget.

 PTA Mission App System (OPPMA) (84T): The United States Naval Observatory (USNO) is responsible for providing Precise Time and Time Interval (PTTI) to the DoD under Joint Chiefs of Staff Instruction
 (CJCSI) 6130.01H in direct support of national strategic priorities. USNO mission application hardware is a highly customized system responsible for the dissemination of PTTI.

 [P40A / Acoustic Positioning System (USBL) Ultra Short Base Line NAVO (85B)]: The Acoustic Positioning System (APS) is capable of performing Super Short Baseline (SSBL) and Long Baseline (LBL) Acoustic
 Positioning used to provide high accuracy navigation and positioning of towed bodies, moored systems and Autonomous Underwater Vehicles (AUVs) deployed from T-AGS 60 oceanographic survey vessels.
 The APS is permanently installed aboard each vessel and supports tracking objects in any direction out to a 5000m radius with a single transponder, or very precise positioning using a series of bottom-mounted
 transponders. In addition, the APS is used to precisely locate lost vehicles or instrumentation for purposes of recovery and to provide acoustic release mechanism and positioning for moored seafloor digital

 acoustic measurement systems. Funding supports these moored systems that are used to collect data to build gridded databases of layered geo-acoustic descriptions of the ocean floor and low frequency
 bottom loss databases, collect fleet anti-submarine warfare support measurements, produce high resolution anti-submarine warfare area assessment products, and provide critical environmental input to acoustic
 transmission loss models.

 [P40A / P-001 Precise Time and Astrometry Network MILCON USNO (84T)]: Funds support the outfitting of a highly customized HEMP-hardened MILCON building with mission equipment such as equipment
 racks, electronics equipment, display monitors and other various furnishings for the Master Clock and Earth Orientation.

 [P40A / OPPMA PTA MISSION APP SYSTEM (84T)]: The United States Naval Observatory (USNO) is responsible for providing Precise Time and Time Interval (PTTI) to the DoD under Joint Chiefs of Staff
 Instruction (CJCSI) 6130.01H in direct support of national strategic priorities. USNO mission application hardware is a highly customized system responsible for the dissemination of PTTI.

 [P40A / OPVLBA - Very Long Baseline Array Resilience (84T) USNO]: The Very Long Baseline Array (VLBA) functions as both input and COOP for all United States Naval Observatory (USNO) Earth Orientation
 Parameter generation. USNO, per CJCSI 6130.01H and DoDI 4650.06, is responsible for the determination and dissemination of Earth Orientation Parameters (EOPs) to the DoD in support of national strategic
 priorities. Navigation assets (e.g. GPS) require knowledge of EOPs in order to calculate position. USNO uses data from radio telescopes around the world and a process called Very Long Baseline Interferometry
 (VLBI) as an essential part of calculating EOPs. Key to this network of telescopes are the ten VLBA antenna. Funding supports the procurement and deployment of Ka-band receivers to address radio frequency
 interference and to replace end of life hardware.

 [P40A / OPPTA - Precise Time and Astrometry USNO (84T)]: Funds provided for tech refreshes, instrumentation upgrades, and modernization of components are crucial for maintaining time and Earth
 Orientation Parameters (EOP) standards in accordance with Joint Chiefs of Staff Instruction (CJCSI) 6130.01H, which dictates USNO responsibility to produce Precise Time and Time Interval (PTTI) data
 products and Earth Orientation Parameters for use by all Department of Defense.

 [P40A / Conductivity Temp Depth (CTD) Optics Pinger System LCR NAVO (85B)]: Funds provided for Life-Cycle Replacement (LCR) parts for the existing T-AGS 60 Class shipboard system with Conductivity,
 Temperature, and Depth CTD-OPTICS-Pinger Systems. Each system is comprised of CTD/Optics underwater unit, CTD Deck Unit, Optics sensors, CTD/Optics sensor custom deployment cage, HIPAP
 transponder (for underwater tracking of the unit), and various electronic components. The costs associated with the CTD-Optics System procurement fluctuates slightly from year to year depending on spares
 procured.

 [P40A / Digital Side Scan Sonar (SHIP) NAVO (85B)]: Funds provided for Digital Side Scan Sonar (SHIP) planned lifecycle upgrade schedule. This is driven by availability from the manufacturer as well as
 emerging requirements. Current systems are unable to meet emerging requirements for high resolution imagery coupled with improved data coverage rates.

 [P40A / Long Term Ambient Noise Recording/Reporting Sys NAVO (85M)]: Long Term Ambient Noise Recording and Reporting System is a moored, acoustic buoy system used to support ISR missions. The
 buoys are four channel Environmental Acoustic Recording System (EARS) units that will record ambient noise for long time periods within an 8kHz bandwidth. The Environmental Acoustic Recording System
 buoys will have to be recovered for data processing

 [P40A / Nav Map Ref Disk and Patrol Analysis System (PBNZ Support)(85M) NAVO]: Funds provided for sustainment of operational production system (Navigation Map Reference Disk and Patrol Analysis
 System - NPAS) used in the creation of strategic products (PBNZ) having direct and significant impact on the ability of Ship Submersible Ballistic, Nuclear (SSBN) assets to operate effectively. NPAS additionally
 supports the creation of critical performance analysis products projected to SSBN assets to enhance operational decision making. Requirement directly supports Secretary of the Navy Instruction (SECNAVINST)
 8120.b and OPNAVINST 8120.1.

 [P40A / Shallow Water Multibeam NAVO (85B)]: The shallow water multibeam sonar system is the primary sea-floor mapping system in littoral waters (10-1000 meters of water). The system collects data used
 for surface and sub-surface littoral navigation charts, high resolution bathymetry required for ocean models.

 [P40A / Ship Davits for Hydro Unmanned Surface Vessel (USV) Systems (85B) NAVO]: New T-AGS ship DAVITS for one (1) vessel to accommodate the Unmanned Surface Vehicle replacements of the manned
 Hydrographic Survey Launches.

 [P40A / Ship Moving Vessel Profiler (MVP) NAVO (85B)]: Funds support lifecycle replacement of Moving Vessel Profiler (MVP) systems primarily due to obsolescence and availability of support from the
 manufacturer. These systems are mechanical in nature and have limited service life in a marine environment.

 [P40A / Ship To Shore Data Comm NAVO (85B)]: Funds provided for Life-Cycle Replacement (LCR) required to replace C-BAND system above deck and below deck equipment. LCR is required to maintain
 system functionality and optimal performance and to meet expanding requirements for remote communications while underway.

 [P40A / (USV) T-AGS Hydrographic Survey Launch, USV Replace NAVO (85B)]: Funds provided to enhance fleet survey capability of creating surface and sub-surface littoral navigation charts. Military surveys
 are increasingly gravitating towards autonomous vehicles and emerging technology survey systems (Mission Equipment Systems) are becoming increasingly tailored for use providing enhanced performance and
 reliability when integrated.

 [P40A / Primary Oceanographic Prediction Sys (POPS)&OIS FNMOC (85H)]: Funds support Department of Defense and Primary Oceanographic Prediction Sys (POPS) guidelines for lifecycle replacement due
 to equipment obsolescence, End-of-Life/End-of-Service (EoL/EoS) issues, and new technology insertion. Replacing the EoL/EoS POPS system components with High Performance Computing (HPC) technology,
 petabyte data storage requirements, and the high-speed network connections between the computing and storage components is a five-year replacement cycle, with 20% of the system components replaced
 each year without increasing the server footprint.

 Conversion of funds affords Fleet Numerical Meteorology and Oceanography Center FNMOC to shift all clusters to include non-HPC infrastructure on all enclaves at their respective lifecycle replacement
 milestones to a fully as-a-service model. To ensure consistent funding remains available to maintain the FNMOC transition to HPCaaS, FNMOC requires a conversion of the POPS MET OPN to OMN. This
 HPCaaS solution eliminates the use of aging hardware & the related cybersecurity findings, decreases redundant IT costs and standardizes/modernizes IT infrastructure.

 [P40A / AUV Advanced Sensors (85M) NAVO]: Advanced sonar sensors such as Synthetic Aperture Sonar (SAS) module. The new sensors will provide high resolution seabed characterization resulting in high
 confidence environmental databases.

 [P40A / Long Term Ambient Noise Recording/Reporting Sys (85M)]: Long Term Ambient Noise Recording and Reporting System is a moored, acoustic buoy system used to support ISR missions. The buoys
 are four channel Environmental Acoustic Recording System (EARS) units that will record ambient noise for long time periods within an 8kHz bandwidth. The Environmental Acoustic Recording System buoys will
 have to be recovered for data processing.

 [P40A / Mine Warfare (MIW) Autonomous Undersea Vehicles (AUVs) (85M) NAVO]: Two man-portable autonomous undersea vehicles (AUVs) plus spares. One (1) AUV System consisting of three (3)
 unmanned undersea vehicles with depth capability of up to three hundred (300) meters for medium depth ocean and littoral environmental battlespace awareness seafloor interrogation.

 [P40A / Multi Sensor Core Logger (MSCL) (85M) NAVO]: Laboratory Multi Sensor Core Logger (MSCL) is the Geology Labs' primary non-destructive analytical instrument which provides high resolution data from
 multiple sensors of ocean bottom sediment cores used to update geophysical and acoustic databases for the Fleet.

 [P40A / NAVIFOR Acoustic Measurement System NAVO (85M)]: Lifecycle replacement and upgrade of digital acoustic measurement systems which provides data used to build gridded databases of layered
 geoacoustic descriptions of the ocean floor, collects anti-submarine warfare support measurements, produces high resolution anti-submarine warfare area assessment products, and serves to provide critical
 environmental input to acoustic transmission loss models. System consists of multi-channel variable depth buoys, shipboard data acquisition, control, and processing support systems. Buoys acquire data,
 provide signal conditioning and gain, and store data in digital form.

 [P40A / Ship Submersible Ballistic (Nuclear) (SSBN) Support NAVO (85M))]: Funds provided to address gaps in existing oceanographic databases which have been identified as having significant impact on the
 ability of Ship Submersible Ballistic, Nuclear (SSBN) assets to operate effectively.

 [P40A / OPPTAN PTA Network (84Z) USNO]: The Precise Time and Astrometry Network (PTA Network) is the network delivering the United States Naval Observatory (USNO) precise time and astrometry
 reference frame to the entire DoD as required by national strategic priorities. This system consists of ten mission enclave subsystems located across three sites in Washington, D.C., Colorado Springs, CO, and
 Flagstaff, AZ. Lifecycle replacements of mission network architecture are in accordance with enclave requirements, is therefore tied to the fiscal year and is consistently executed to budget.

 [P40A / Shallow Water Seismic System NAVO (85M)]: Life Cycle Replacement for Shallow Water Seismic System.

 [P40A / Oceanographic Information Ships (OIS) Tech Refresh-NAVO (85H)]: Oceanographic Information Ships (OIS)-ship Hardware Life Cyle Replacement (LCR) for all Oceanographic Survey Ships to meet
 Information Assurance/Maintenance/Mission requirements - Servers, network switches, printers, plotters, etc.

 [P40A / Oceanographic Ships Virtualization Technology Refresh- NAVO-85H]: Funding this requirement ensures that the oceanographic survey ship collection and processing of data that is provided to the US
 Navy is accurate meteorological and oceanographic forecast and reports to support worldwide fleet operations. Supports safety of navigation for submarines and surface ships.

 [P40A / Virtual Storage Demilitarized Zone Life Cycle Replacement-NAVO-85H]: Procurement supports replacement of all A2 chipset virtual computer cluster storage devices (Non-Classified Internet Protocol
 Router Network (NIPR) Demilitarized Zone (DMZ), Secret Internet Protocol Router Network (SIPR) DMZ, and Disaster Recovery) supporting dissemination of oceanography data and products. The A2 virtual
 compute cluster consists of the physical host servers and storage used by oceanography commands to host applications, process, and disseminate data. All server based operations are dependent on the
 A2 virtual cluster infrastructure. The virtual servers support numerous critical organizational processes. Functions supported include: Oceanographic Survey Ships data ingest, external data receipt, data
 dissemination, gliders, real-time observation processing, and ocean modeling processing are a few of the critical organizational processes. The organizational mission areas include: safety of navigation, Anti-
 Submarine Warfare (ASW), Mine Warfare (MIW), and Expeditionary Warfare (EXW). The virtual clusters also support key cybersecurity/IA and systems management resiliency capabilities such as Assured
 Compliance Assessment Solution (ACAS), Domain Controllers, systems management servers, and Disaster Recovery (DR).

 [P40A / Virtual Cluster Technology Refresh-NAVO-85H]: A2 computer chipset Virtual Cluster Hardware:
 The purpose of this procurement is to replace all of A2 virtual compute clusters, (Non-Classified Internet Protocol Router Network (NIPR) Enclave, NIPR Demilitarized Zone (DMZ), Secret Internet Protocol
 Router Network (SIPR) and SIPR DMZ). The A2 virtual compute cluster consists of the physical host servers and networking equipment used by Fleet Numerical Meteorology and Oceanography Center
 (FNMOC) model operations and Naval Oceanographic Office (NAVO) production codes to host applications and process data. All server based operations are dependent on the A2 virtual cluster infrastructure.
 The A2 virtual clusters are comprised of networking gear and server hosts which support over 300 virtual servers across the different environments (Enclave and DMZ) and NIPR/SIPR classifications. The virtual
 servers support numerous critical organizational processes.

 Functions supported include: Oceanography Survey Ships data ingest, external data receipt National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA), data dissemination, real-time processing, FNMOC ocean
 model pre-processing, and FNMOC ocean modeling post-processing are a few of the critical organizational processes.

 Mission areas include: safety of navigation, Anti-Submarine Warfare (ASW), Mine Warfare (MIW), and Expeditionary Warfare (EXW). The virtual clusters also support key cybersecurity/Information Assurance (IA)
 and systems management resiliency capabilities such as Assured Compliance Assessment Solution (ACAS), Domain Controllers, systems management servers, and Disaster Recovery (DR).

 [P40A / Life Cycle Replacement Firewall & Cyber Appliances HW-NAVO-85H]: The requirement is to Life Cycle Replacement (LCR) existing Network Firewalls and Intrusion Detection/Protection System
 appliances reaching End of Life (EOL) on the Primary Oceanographic Prediction System, Oceanography (POPS-OCN). Hardware Tech Refresh for these systems is compulsory and essential to meet
 the operational mission of POPS-OCN. The appliances directly support and sustain POPS-OCN cyber security processes for the Command, US Navy, Combatant Commander with accurate and secure
 meteorological and oceanographic products in support of operational armed forces; and enabling Cyber Defense Operations requirements.

 Life Cycle Replacement of 4X Cyber Firewall appliances required for Unclassified and Classified networks; LCR of 2X cyber switches required. LCR of NIPR and SIPR Intrusion Detection/Protection Systems
 (3X) required to sustain strategic sensor grid of Navy Cyber Defense Operations in support of cybersecurity operations"

 [P40A / Life Cycle Replacement (LCR) Ship-to-Shore (S2S) Network HW-NAVO-85H]: The requirement is for Life Cycle Replacement (LCR) of the Ship-to-shore (S2S) network equipment which enables secure
 communication links between forward deployed survey ships and the operational data center. The S2S network equipment provides survey data transfer, voice communications and access to operational
 data center and external resources crucial for at-sea operations in support of the numbered fleet commander under Tactical Control (TACON) and the oceanographic production of combatant commander's
 deliverables. Missions include: navigation, Autonomous Underwater Vehicle (AUV)/ unmanned underwater vehicles (UUV), hydrographic, bathymetric, oceanographic and Special Missions.

 LCR of 23X Layer3 Routers required to sustain cyber compliance for Risk Management Framework (RMF)/High Risk Environment (HRE); Designated routers include: 6 Oceanographic Survey Ships C-Band (2
 per ship) = 12; 2 Satellite Data Processing (SDP) (Naval Computer & Telecommunications Area Master Station (NCTAMS)/Satellite Communications (SATCOM) peering 2 per site) = 4; 1 Naval Oceanographic
 Office (Defense Information Systems Network (DISN) Circuit Point of Presence (PoP) 2 units) = 2; 5 internal Secret Internet Protocol Router Network (SIPR) tunneling routers. Enterprise Mission Assurance
 Support Service (EMASS) Authorizations to Operate (ATOs): 2495, 3227, 4173.

 [P40A / Domain Management Systems-NAVO-85H]: Provides centralized management capabilities for the scientific network to include account management (authentication/authorization), systems management
 functions (patching, license management), email systems, domain name system (DNS), centralized logging systems, cybersecurity vulnerability scanners, host-based intrusion detection (HBSS), and antivirus
 systems. Capabilities are crucial to ensure cybersecurity compliance and operations of the network.

 [P40A / High Performance Computing (HPC) Technology Refresh-NAVO-85H]: "Procurement replaces all of the components for the High Performance Computing (HPC) cluster. The HPC cluster has reached
 End of Life (EOL) and technical refresh of the system is required to meet the operational mission and to maintain cyber security compliance. The HPC cluster consists of physical servers, networking equipment,
 and storage used by various groups at the Naval Oceanographic Office. The physical servers consist of compute cluster scheduling systems, high performance processing systems, and cluster management
 systems.
 Functions supported include; Naval Oceanographic Satellite Data Processing (SDP) team import and processing of satellite data and Multi-Channel Sea Surface Temperature (MCSST) data, Data storage and
 processing to support fleet operations to include Anti-submarine Warfare (ASW), Mine Warfare (MIW), Surface Warfare (SUW), Naval Special Warfare (NSW), Non-Combatant Evacuation Operations (NEO),
 Undersea Warfare (USW), Amphibious Warfare (AMW), and Expeditionary Warfare (EXW)."

 [P40A / Data Center Network Switches-Life Cycle Replacement (LCR)-NAVO-85H]: The requirement is for the Life Cycle Replacement (LCR) of 18 Data Center network switches for the classified and
 unclassified networks. The network equipment provides cyber security capabilities to maintain compliance with Authority to Operate (ATO) and Risk Management Framework (RMF) requirements. The functional
 replacement will provide/sustain the connectivity requirements for IT computing and storage resources that enable the scientific information technology business processes and production to meet the product(s)
 and battlespace information demands of the combatant commander, services, agencies and field activities (CC/S/A/FA).

 [P40A / Network Services Life Cycle Replacement Distributed Switches-NAVO-85H]: The requirement is for lifecycle replacement network equipment distributed across the scientific networks. The network
 components provide IT and cyber security (CS) connectivity services enabling the scientific user community on Unclassified and Classified networks supporting Mission Essential Functions (oceanographic
 and meteorological products) including oceanographic data collection, modeling, product generation and data dissemination to support operational requirements and battlespace information demands of the
 combatant commander, services, agencies and field activities. Mission Areas include: Safety of Navigation/ Surface Warfare (SUW), Naval Special Warfare (NSW), Non-Combatant Evacuation Operations
 (NEO), Undersea Warfare (USW), Amphibious Warfare (AMW), Anti Air Warfare (AAW), and the Intelligence Community (IC). The requirement includes network components hardware and software; Sustainment
 maintenance covered under the CISCO "GEMSS" contract. (U) The network components (Local Area Network (LAN) switches) for the Distributed Access LAN supporting the unclassified and classified enclave
 are end of life and cyber support in FY23. The Network Access Control appliances for cyber-802.1X network authentication (Risk Management Framework Category 1 requirement) and the Service Delivery Point
 Router for Classified telecommunications circuits are end-of life and cyber support in FY24. Replacement is compulsory to sustain IT operations and Authority to Operate (ATO).

 [P40A / Integrated Survey Systems-60 Software Maintenance (OIS)-NAVO-85H]: Support for the OIS follow-on of the services offered on the Integrated Survey Systems Oceanographic Survey Ship (ISS-60).
 This system software suite must continue to be tested, upgraded and maintained for interoperability and functionality with new non-IT mission sensors, such as next generation shallow water multi-beam sonars,
 digital side scan sonar systems, and high precision military global positioning system receivers. The ISS-60 survey software acquires, stores, displays, and processes oceanographic and meteorological data
 collected by various mission systems using a fiber-optic network configuration. ISS-60 survey software is a critical component in the Naval Oceanographic Office's (NAVOCEANO) ability to collect and integrate
 oceanographic information in support of surface and subsurface navigation as well as provide environmental characterization of the Battlespace.

 [P40A / Storage Area Network (SAN) LCR-NAVO-85H]: The requirement is for the replacement of the IT hardware components for the Storage Area Network (SAN) and includes servers and storage devices
 (tape drives, disk arrays, tapes). The SAN hardware and software is end-of-life/support. This results in numerous high risk cybersecurity vulnerabilities across networks (Non-Classified Internet Protocol Router
 Network (NIPR) and Secret Internet Protocol Router Network (SIPR)) that cannot be mitigated or resolved without procurement. The life cycle replacement has been deferred since 2019 creating significant
 operational risk as oceanography data collection, model processing, product generation are dependent on the SAN. Includes Surveys, unmanned underwater vehicles (UUVs), product generation, data
 dissemination, model processing, data storage, and disaster recovery (DR). Mission areas supported by oceanography data: Surface Warfare (SUW), Naval Special Warfare (NSW), Non-Combatant Evacuation
 Operations (NEO), Undersea Warfare (USW), Amphibious Warfare (AMW), Anti Air Warfare (AAW), safety of navigation, Anti-Submarine Warfare (ASW), Mine Warfare (MIW), and Expeditionary Warfare (EXW).
 New FY25 requirement joint letter from United States Fleet Forces Command (USFFC) and United States Pacific Fleet (USPACFLT) will require the Naval Oceanographic Office to be the bathymetry (the
 measurement of water depth) repository for the Joint Force with more data being ingested will result in additional storage resources required.

 [P40A / Primary Oceanographic Prediction Sys (POPS)&OIS NAVIFOR HQ (85H)]: This is for Naval Meteorology and Oceanography Command Cross Domain Solution 85F because they cannot execute OPN
 funding.
 The Naval Meteorology and Oceanography Command (NMOC) Cross Domain Solution (CDS) supports the mission requirement to provide 24x7 global weather and ocean model prediction products, weather
 satellite imagery products, and related tactical decision aids and data in support of Naval, Joint, and Coalition operations worldwide. These mission products support virtually every combat platform and weapon
 system operated by the U.S. Navy and are crucial for ensuring the safety of naval ships and aircraft. Trusted Gateway Systems (TGS) (specializes in image files) and High-Speed Guard (HSG) (processes
 all non-image files) systems at Fleet Numerical Meteorology and Oceanography Center (FNMOC), Monterey, CA (14,100 Gigabytes (GB) per day and TGS system at Naval Oceanographic Office, Stennis
 Space Center, MS (2,475 GB per day). The TGS system provides high- bandwidth/high-throughput one-way oceanographic and meteorologic data transfers from the Non-Classified Internet Protocol Router
 Network (NIPRNET) to the Secret Internet Protocol Router Network (SIPRNET) and Joint Worldwide Intelligence Communications System (JWICS) enabling on-time data assimilation, processing, analysis, and
 dissemination, information, and products on classified enclaves enabling battlespace awareness and decision superiority for afloat and ashore Navy, Joint, Allied and Coalition Forces. Must be in compliance with
 the National Security Agency (NSA) Raise the Bar requirements.

 Justification:
 Ship Moving Vessel Profiler (MVP) NAVO (85B)
 FY26 funding supports the procurement of one (1) Moving Vessel Profiler (MVP) systems in order to complete life cycle replacement.

 Hydrographic Survey Launch (HSL) Unmanned Surface Vessels (USV)
 FY26 funding supports updated integrated mission hardware for the lifecycle replacement of Unmanned Surface Vessels (USV) with Launch and Recovery System (LARS) for hydrographic data collection
 requirements.

 Ship Davits for Hydro Unmanned Surface Vessel (USV) Systems (85B) NAVO
 FY26 funding supports procurement of one (1) Universal Ship Davit to support Unmanned Surface Vessel (USV) Operations.

 Conductivity, Temperature, and Depth (CTD) Optics Pinger System LCR
 FY26 funding supports lifecycle replacement parts for required lifecycle replacement of one (1) shipboard system. Conductivity, Temperature, and Depth (CTD) OPTICS-Pinger Systems are a life cycle
 replacement of the existing T-AGS 60 Class shipboard systems. Each system is comprised of a CTD Optics underwater unit, CTD Deck Unit, Optics sensors, CTD/Optics sensor custom deployment cage, High
 Precision Acoustic Underwater Positioning (HIPAP) transponder (for underwater tracking of the unit), and various electronic components.

 Primary Oceanographic Prediction Sys (POPS) & Oceanography Information System (OIS)(85H)
 FY26 funding supports replacing the End of Life/End of Service POPS system components with High Performance Computing (HPC) technology, petabyte data storage requirements, and the high-speed network
 connections between the computing and storage components is both complicated and expensive.

 Other Procurement Precise Time and Astrometry (OPPTA) (84T)
 FY26 funds support tech refreshes, instrumentation upgrades, and modernization of components crucial for maintaining Master Clock and Earth Orientation Parameters (EOP) standards in accordance with Joint
 Chiefs of Staff Instruction (CJCSI) 6130.01H, which dictates USNO responsibility to produce Precise Time and Time Interval (PTTI) data products and Earth Orientation Parameters for use by all Department of
 Defense in support of national strategic priorities.

 OTHER PROCUREMENT VERY LONG BASELINE ARRAY RESILIENCE (84T)
 FY26 funds support the lifecycle replacement of three Ka-band receivers out of the ten VLBA sites in the United States.

 Other Procurement Precise Time and Astrometry Network (OPPTAN) (84Z)
 FY26 funding supports procurement of equipment modernization necessary to maintain the required security posture and ensure PTA network availability.

 Mine Warfare (MIW) Autonomous Undersea Vehicles (AUVs) (85M) NAVO

 Footnotes:
 (1)
       Decrease of $607K due to completion of MILCON migration.
 (2)
    The Very Long Baseline Array (VLBA) functions as both input and COOP for all United States Naval Observatory (USNO) Earth Orientation Parameter generation. USNO, per CJCSI 6130.01H and DoDI 4650.06, is responsible for the
 determination and dissemination of Earth Orientation Parameters (EOPs) to the DoD in support of national strategic priorities. Navigation assets (e.g. GPS) require knowledge of EOPs in order to calculate position. USNO uses data from
 radio telescopes around the world and a process called Very Long Baseline Interferometry (VLBI) as an essential part of calculating EOPs. Key to this network of telescopes are the ten VLBA antenna. Funding supports the procurement and
 deployment of Ka-band receivers to address radio frequency interference and to replace end of life hardware.
 (3)
       Increase of $685K supports the procurement of Two Way Satellite Time Transfer (TWSTT) Portable Link System.
 (4)
       Increase of $83K due to cost inflation to support LCR for T-AGS 60 CTD Optics Pinger System to include install and testing support.
 (5)
       Since funded in FY25, not needed in FY26. Next LCR is FY31.
 (6)
       Funded in FY25. Next LCR is FY30 in a 5 year interval.
 (7)
       Supports procurement of one (1) Universal Ship Davit to support Unmanned Surface Vessel (USV) Operations. Increase is due to nothing planned for FY25, only FY26.

 Description:
 This line item procures equipment to support the security and safety for the Department's assets worldwide.

 Physical Security/Access Control (PS/AC) supports the mission to deter, detect and defeat threats at the outermost perimeter, protecting air assets and high value units. Access control points systems are critical
 to providing Force Protection at the Navy's Continental United States (CONUS) installations. Effective and reliable barriers are the best system for keeping unauthorized vehicles and personnel from entering the
 base.

 P40A / Visual Augmentation Systems (VAS): The United States Navy Service Common Visual Augmentation Systems (VAS) Program of Record manages, procures, and maintains night vision devices, thermal
 detection devices, day/night weapons optics, and lasers in support of Navy combat capabilities with regard to the detection, recognition, classification, tracking, and destruction of hostile air and surface forces.
 The USN VAS Program also manages research into the future of visual augmentation systems and engages with Navy and DoD VAS stakeholders to ensure the Navy maintains competitive advantage over near-
 peer adversaries.

 Other Procurement, Navy funding provides the United States Navy Service Common Visual Augmentation Systems (VAS) Program of Record with funding to procure new and replacement night vision
 equipment, thermal detection equipment, day/night optics and associated test equipment for ships, submarines, carriers, expeditionary forces and shore installations so that commanders can maintain situational
 awareness in degraded visual environments while maintaining the greatest possible distance from threats.

 Counter small Unmanned Aerial Systems (C-sUAS) Family of Systems (FoS) program was funded to provide initial material solutions to SECNAV-prioritized locations CONUS and Outside the Continental United
 States (OCONUS) with baseline electronic warfare (EW) capabilities to detect, track, identify, and defeat Unmanned Aerial Systems (UAS) over a large coverage area. Following initial material deployment,
 the program continues to expand the capabilities of select, high priority systems and adapt new solutions to defeat evolving threats. The initial material solution included Counter-RCMA Integrated Air Defense
 Network (CORIAN), Ninja, DroneDefender, and DroneBuster. Expanded solutions include Electro Optical/ Infrared (EO/IR), radar, Command and Control (C2), new EW detect/defeat capabilities, alternative
 sensors, and additional kinetic and non-kinetic mitigations needed to prepare, maintain, and upkeep force protection, self-defense and operational capabilities against the ever-evolving UAS threat. Funding is
 provided to protect high-value and/or critical Navy assets and support public safety against threats by inadvertent or unlawful misuse of UAS.

 [P40A / SSBN WATERFRONT RESTRICTED AREA SECURITY (WRAS)]: Funding provides for the security equipment required to guard and protect the TRIDENT II (D5) missile while the missile is in storage,
 being handled, or in a movement convoy to and from the waterfront at the Strategic Weapons Facility, Atlantic (SWFLANT) in Kings Bay (KB), GA and the Strategic Weapons Facility, Pacific (SWFPAC) in

1810N: Other Procurement, Navy / BA 07: Personnel & Command Support Equip /                                  8128 / Physical Security Equipment

ID Code (A=Service Ready, B=Not Service Ready): A                       Program Elements for Code B Items: N/A                                   Other Related Program Elements: N/A
Line Item MDAP/MAIS Code: N/A
 Bangor, WA. Funding supports the Waterfront Restricted Area and Limited Area (LA) Electronic Security Systems (ESS) installations and refreshes, Vehicle Barriers, the Command, Control, Communication,
 Computers, and Intelligence (C4I) system that provides security system, communication network, computers, security force command and control function as well as the Water Detection System and other
 equipment necessary to meet Nuclear Security requirements.

 [P40A / VISUAL AUGMENTATION SYSTEMS (VAS)]: The United States Navy Service Common Visual Augmentation Systems (VAS) Program of Record manages, procures, and maintains night vision devices,
 thermal detection devices, day/night weapons optics, and lasers in support of Navy combat capabilities with regard to the detection, recognition, classification, tracking, and destruction of hostile air and surface
 forces. The USN VAS Program also manages research into the future of visual augmentation systems and engages with Navy and DoD VAS stakeholders to ensure the Navy maintains competitive advantage
 over near-peer adversaries. Funding is provided to procure new and replacement night vision equipment, thermal detection equipment, day/night optics and associated test equipment for ships, submarines,
 carriers, expeditionary forces and shore installations so that commanders can maintain situational awareness in degraded visual environments while maintaining the greatest possible distance from threats.

 [P40A / Night Vision, Individual (AN/PVS-31B)]: Funds provided for binocular night vision devices to support low-/no-light naval operations.

 [P40A / NVD Helmet Mount]: Funds provided for mounting devices to attach the binocular night vision devices to the helmet.

 [P40A / TAC]: Funds provided for Transportation Account Code (TAC) to support shipment OEM to/from depot.

 [P40A / ANTI-TERRORISM AFLOAT (ATA) EQUIPAGE]: Anti-Terrorism/Force Protection (ATFP) Physical Security Equipment (PSE) material is a compilation of specific security and AT related items aligned
 with Chief of Naval Operations (CNO's) objective for operation of watch standers pier side, at perimeter posts, and underway to assist shipboard security forces in thwarting potential terrorist attacks. Visit
 Boarding Search and Seizure (VBSS) material enables surface forces to reach full Maritime Interception Operations (MIO) capability including interception, boarding, searching, diverting and /or seizing suspect
 vessels.

 [P40A / Next Generation Maritime Security Helmet (Enhanced Combat Helmet)]: Funding supports Next Generation Maritime Security Helmets which provide comprehensive ballistic protection to increase
 survivability during shipboard ATFP, exposed battle station, and VBSS missions. Helmet quantity also decreased to enable shortened replacement interval for armor due to accelerated deterioration of vests/
 plates versus helmets. Helmet service life deemed to be twice that of armor based on actual material condition of equipment after four years of use.

 [P40A / Maritime Armor System]: Funding supports Maritime Armor Systems (MAS) and Naval Security Forces (NSF) Tactical Vests which provide comprehensive ballistic protection to increase survivability
 during shipboard ATFP, exposed battle station, and VBSS missions. Increasing quantities are required for the increased replacement interval of armor due to accelerated deterioration of vests/plates versus
 helmets.

 [P40A / Maritime Buoyant Plates]: Funding supports Maritime Buoyant Plates (MBP) which provide comprehensive ballistic protection to increase survivability of sailors during shipboard ATFP, exposed battle
 station, and VBSS missions.

 [P40A / Enhanced Small Arms Protective Inserts]: Funds provided for Enhanced Small Arms Protective Inserts that provide protection against high intensity rifle threats.

 [P40A / Production Engineering]: Funding provided for biometrics capability to the Fleet surface ships for Visit, Board, Search and Seizure (VBSS) Enhanced Maritime Interception Operations (EMIO) by providing
 Maritime Domain Awareness (MDA) capability to download fused terrorism intelligence. Funding procures software licenses necessary to maintain software and cybersecurity posture for the systems.

 [P40A / Biometrically Enabled Access Control - Husbanding (BEACH)]: Funding supports baseline production and production engineering support for the Biometrically Enabled Access Control - Husbanding
 (BEACH). BEACH is a two-factor authentication capability to improve identity and authentication assurance for entrance to CONUS and OCONUS Navy-Controlled and non-Navy-Controlled ports.

 [P40A / NAVY NON-LETHAL EFFECTS (NNLE)]: Navy Non-Lethal Effects (NNLE) is a Family of Systems (FoS) used to determine the hostile intent of vessels approaching High Value Assets or secure areas.
 There are three (3) systems in the FoS: Acoustic Hailing Device (AHD), used to verbally/audibly hail approaching vessels using a Commercial-Off-The-Shelf product; Long Range Ocular Interrupter (LROI),

 a non-lethal laser dazzler used to hail/warn/suppress approaching vessels; and Maritime Vessel Stopping (MVS), a pneumatic launchable material that blocks the approaching vessel's prop to significantly
 reduce speed. The LROI-Combined Ocular Interrupter Laser (COIL) procurement will be fielded to replace the currently fielded obsolete device and provide significantly enhanced and governmentally developed
 capability that will extend the range and increase the usage of the capability into the daylight hours.

 [P40A / NAVAL SPECIAL WARFARE]: Naval Special Warfare (NSW) service-common funding provides mission critical equipment to U.S. Navy SEAL combat operations in forward deployed contingency
 locations. These investments for visual augmentation systems include but are not limited to weapon laser range finders, optics and illuminators. This funding also supports the latest EO/IR sensors and night
 vision goggles for NSW personnel. This materiel supports NSW core mission requirements and is essential to the readiness and battlefield effectiveness of NSW in winning the current fight against global
 terrorist organizations as well as near-peer competitors.

 [P40A / Aimpoint FCS13 (M3E1)]: Funds provided for fire control systems for the Multi-Role Anti-Armor Anti-Personnel Weapon Systems.

 [P40A / TAC]: Funds provided for Transportation Account Code (TAC) to support shipment OEM to/from depot.

 [P40A / Public Safety Systems; formerly ANTI-TERRORISM FORCE PROTECTION ASHORE]: Funding provides centrally procured equipment to improve the physical security posture of Navy installations
 worldwide. The program applies the Commander Navy Installations Command (CNIC) Risk-based investment strategy, ensuring appropriate Anti-Terrorism and Force Protection (ATFP) solutions are fielded.
 The Physical Security Equipment (PSE) program procures equipment that supports and improves capabilities to detect, defer, and defeat terrorist and criminal activity targeted against Navy personnel,
 government property and facilities ashore/afloat. The program provides funds to procure equipment for Navy Military Construction (MILCON) projects, including Intrusion Detection System(s) (IDS) and other
 Electronic Security System(s) (ESS) before building occupancy. The funds support the following six categories: Electronic Harbor Security Systems (EHSS) and Barriers; Physical Security/Access Control (Gates
 Automation & Perimeter Security); MILCON IDS; Command, Control, Computer, Communications & Intelligence (C4I) and Other Physical Security Equipment (PSE).

 [P40A / ELECTRONIC HARBOR SECURITY SYSTEMS (EHSS)]: Funds provided for Electronic Harbor Security Systems (EHSS) used for the detection and assessment of the potential threats.

 [P40A / Physical Security Access Control Installation Perimeter Access Control]: Funds provided for enhanced physical security posture and reduces manpower at unmanned pedestrian access control
 points. Additionally, funding supports remote monitoring, communications, and lock down, Defense Biometric Identification System (DBIDS) credential authentication at perimeter locations, Lenel credential
 authentication at internal enclave locations and provides crash-rated physical security posture and reduction of unauthorized vehicular access at installation access control points.

 [P40A / Physical Security Access Control Enclaves Electronic Security Systems]: Funds provided for additional layers of security, including secure ingress and egress, to a perimeter or area of interest
 requiring an enhanced security posture currently not available to support aircrafts (FA-18C, FA-18E/F, C-40). Enclave access control, intrusion detection, and video management systems supporting Vehicle
 automated access control point (ACP-A) for bi-directional locations, Navy Munitions Command Security System, Radar and/or EO/IR sensors, Command Control Television (CCTV) (daylight and uncooled
 thermal) assessment, Local sensor and video management server, Data over local N6 wired and/or secure wireless networks, Connected to Region through N6 PS-Net, Open S/W architecture for scalability,
 Standard data / video compression techniques, Integrate command & control via Physical Security Information Module (PSIM) with Local Dispatch Centers (LDC)/Regional Dispatch Centers (RDC) facilities,
 "Dispatchable" Alerts/Alarms sent to E-NERMS via LAMAS (or direct to CERS for OCONUS), and Commonality of enclave enterprise architecture design with other accredited NAVFAC ACP-A systems for
 long-term support and sustainment. Improves physical security at three Fleet facilities around pier enclaves through limited perimeter surveillance, sensor analytics, and remote situational awareness. Provide
 detection/surveillance capability at Entry Control Points (ECPs), Alarm Points (Fence), and waterside perimeter. Command Control Television (CCTV) (daylight and uncooled thermal) assessment, Local sensor
 and video management server. Connected to Region through N6 PS-Net, open software (S/W) architecture for scalability, standard data/video compression techniques, integrate command & control via PSIM
 Sensor viewing, analytics, and management functions controlled from Local Dispatch Center (LDC) and/or Regional Dispatch Center (RDC). Provide video surveillance, situational awareness 24/7 and reduces
 enclave manning requirements.

 [P40A / MILITARY CONSTRUCTION INTRUSION DETECTION SYSTEMS (MILCON IDS)]: Funding supports the procurement of additional MILCON IDS to allow more structures to comply with DoD minimum
 anti-terrorism standards for buildings.

 [P40A / COMMAND, CONTROL, COMPUTER, COMMUNICATIONS AND INTELLIGENCE (C4I)]: Funding support additional upgrades to Regional Dispatch Centers that provide regionalized/consolidated
 emergency call receipt, alarm monitoring, and 1st responder dispatching, Utilize centrally-hosted and supported Computer Aided Dispatch, Mobile Data Computers, Records Management Systems, Geographic
 Information System (GIS), Enterprise Land Mobile Radio (ELMR), and Alarm Automation to optimize emergency communication and response times.

 [P40A / Next Generation 911 (NG911)]: FY26 increase ($24.677M) Next Generation 911 (NG911) Change to the Program: Procures and sustains NG911 dispatch systems to ensure compliance with federal
 law as well as several DoD directives and instructions. Outcome: Allows integration of caller information and location data into public safety answering point (PSAP) and dispatch solution in compliance with: (a)
 Federal Law 47 CFR 9.16(b)(1) and 47 CFR 9.3 (b) DODI 6055.17 (c) DODI 2000.12 (d) SECNAV 3400.4(e) OPNAVINST 3440.17A.

 [P40A / Physical Security Recapitalization]: FY26 increase ($7.955M) for Physical Security Recapitalization Change to the Program: Adds OPN funding to address shortfalls in Navy Electronic Security Systems
 (ESS) Centrally Managed Program. Outcome: Funds NAV ESS Centrally Managed Program establishment (organic engineering expertise), ESS RDTE to align with SECNAV and N4 policy, and address high
 priority shore program priorities (Classified Material Storage Areas (CMSAs), AA&E Storage areas, Perimeter Protection).

 [P40A / Navy Construction Forces (NCF) FoSUAS Systems]: Funding supports the procurement of additional solution sets (multiple Type/Model/Series (T/M/S) with varying quantities of each) to provide a more
 robust family of
 systems and fulfill a broader scope of capability requirements as addressed in the Navy FoSUAS Top Level Requirement (TLR). Funding procures existing and emerging SUAS technologies, mission kits, and
 payloads in Fixed Wing
 (FW) and Vertical Take Off and Landing (VTOL) solution sets for Reconnaissance, Surveillance, and Target Acquisition (RSTA). FoSUAS includes Short Range/Short Endurance (SR/SE) and Medium Range/
 Medium Endurance
 (MR/ME) categories of Small Unmanned Aircraft Systems (SUAS) with each category tailored toward specific mission needs/ profiles. As such, a mix of T/M/S SUAS makes up the FoSUAS Program of Record
 (PoR) as addressed
 in the Navy's FoSUAS TLR document.

 [P40A / Physical Security Water Barrier]: Funding provides for equipment to improve the physical security posture of Navy installations worldwide. The Physical Security Equipment (PSE) program procures
 equipment that supports and improves capabilities to detect, defer, and defeat terrorist and criminal activity targeted against Navy personnel, government property and facilities ashore/afloat. This ensures
 appropriate Anti-terrorism and Force Protection (ATFP) solutions are fielded.

Note: Totals in this Exhibit P-40 set may not be exact or sum exactly due to rounding.

 Justification:
 The FY 2026 request was reduced by $1.962 million for civilian personnel to optimize the workforce in compliance with Executive Order 14210, "Implementing the President's Department of Government
 Efficiency Workforce Optimization Initiative."

 SSBN Waterfront Restricted Area Security (WRAS)
 FY26 increase ($2.730M) due to the refresh of the Sense and Interdiction (SENSEI) Ashore suite of sensors. Funding will be used to procure new sensors with increased performance to replace the legacy radar
 and optical sensors currently fielded. These new sensors will be integrated within the existing SENSEI Ashore System. This will allow for future expansion to integrate additional defeat capabilities in the areas
 of C-UAS hardware and software refresh; electromagnetic effects testing; and radiation hazards testing to keep pace with the evolving Un-crewed Aircraft System (C-UAS) threat while remaining operationally
 relevant. Additionally, there is a refresh for Near-Port Security Barriers (N-PSBs) beginning in FY26 at the Strategic Weapons Facilities. N-PSBs are floating barriers used to protect vessels, ports, and critical
 infrastructure from attacks by small, high-speed vessels.

 Night Vision, Individual (AN/PVS-31B)
 FY26 increase of ($0.657M) due to the modernization of the VAS program. The United States Navy Service Common Visual Augmentation Systems (VAS) Program of Record manages, procures, and maintains
 night vision devices, thermal detection devices, day/night weapons optics, and lasers in support of Navy combat capabilities with regard to the detection, recognition, classification, tracking, and destruction of
 hostile air and surface forces. The USN VAS Program also manages research into the future of visual augmentation systems and engages with Navy and DoD VAS stakeholders to ensure the Navy maintains
 competitive advantage over near-peer adversaries. Funding provided to procure new and replacement night vision equipment, thermal detection equipment, day/night optics and associated test equipment for
 ships, submarines, carriers, expeditionary forces and shore installations so that commanders can maintain situational awareness in degraded visual environments while maintaining the greatest possible distance
 from threats.

 Anti-Terrorism Afloat (ATA) Equipage
 FY26 increase ($0.188M) for economic cost adjustments of Armor System (MAS), Maritime Buoyant Plates (MBP), and Enhanced Small Arms Protective Inserts.

 Biometrically Enabled Access Control - Husbanding (BEACH)
 FY26 increase ($0.747M) supports increased production and production engineering support for BEACH. BEACH is a two-factor authentication capability to improve identity and authentication assurance for
 entrance to CONUS and OCONUS Navy-Controlled and non-Navy- Controlled ports.

 Navy Non-Lethal Effects (NNLE)
 FY26 funds continuation of Long Range Ocular Interrupter (LROI) - Combined Ocular Interrupter Laser (COIL) procurement. No change in funding.

 NAVSCIATTS FoSUAS Systems
 FY26 decrease ($1.040M) to support higher Department priorities

Appropriation / Budget Activity / Budget Sub Activity:                                                     P-1 Line Item Number / Title:
1810N: Other Procurement, Navy / BA 07: Personnel & Command Support Equip /                                8128 / Physical Security Equipment
BSA 2: Command Support Equipment
ID Code (A=Service Ready, B=Not Service Ready): A                      Program Elements for Code B Items: N/A                                 Other Related Program Elements: N/A
Line Item MDAP/MAIS Code: N/A

 Naval Special Warfare
 NSW Family of Small Unmanned Aircraft Systems (FoSUAS)
 FY26 decrease ($6.032M) to support higher Department priorities

 Navy Construction Force (NCF) FoSUAS
 FY26 decrease ($2.118M) due to prioritization of funding to sustain fielded systems

 Public Safety Systems (N6S) (Formerly ATFP)
 FY26 increase ($0.903M) supports procurement of equipment for the improvement of physical security posture of Navy installations.

 FY26 increase ($1.357M) due to C4I upgrade requirement completion. Funding supports procurement of equipment for the physical security posture of Navy installations. Funding supports additional upgrades
 to Regional Dispatch Centers who provide regionalized/consolidated emergency call receipt, alarm monitoring, and 1st responder dispatching, utilize centrally hosted and supported Computer Aided Dispatch,
 Mobile Data Computers, Records Management Systems, Geographic Information System (GIS), Enterprise Land Mobile Radio (ELMR), and Alarm Automation to optimize emergency communication and
 response times.

 FY26 increase ($24.677M) due to change to Program Next Generation 911 (NG911) procures NG911dispatch systems to ensure compliance with federal law as well as several DoD directives and instructions.
 Outcome: Allows integration of caller information and location data into public safety answering point (PSAP) and dispatch solution in compliance with: (a) Federal Law 47CFR ? 9.16(b)(1) and 47 CFR ? 9.3 (b)
 DODI 6055.17 (c) DODI 2000.12 (d) SECNAV 3400.4(e) OPNAVINST 3440.17A.

 FY26 increase ($7.955M) due to change to Program Physical Security Recapitalization funding to address shortfalls in Navy Electronic Security Systems (ESS) Centrally Managed Program. Outcome: Funds
 NAV ESS Centrally Managed Program establishment(organic engineering expertise), ESS RDTE to align with SECNAV and N4 policy, and address high priority shore program priorities (Classified Material
 Storage Areas (CMSAs), AA&E Storage areas, Perimeter Protection).

 Physical Security Water Barrier
 FY26 increase ($0.137M) supports Waterborne Security Barrier (WSB) integral assets within force protection configurations.

 Countering Small Unmanned Aerial Systems (C-sUAS)
 FY26 increase ($10.169M) provides for the procurement, integration and installation as necessary for a partial tech refresh/ replacement for several of the current, aging FoS components in order to maintain
 mission requirements. The current C-sUAS FoS is comprised of aging Electronic Warfare (EW) systems and network components. It has hardware components reaching end-of-life in FY25 with critical
 components no longer able to be repaired or replaced. This increase is a short-term fix to address the most pressing obsolescence issues of the systems and will extend the systems end-of-life. The FY 2026
 request for C-UAS includes $29.632M of discretionary and $232M of mandatory (reconciliation) of a total of $261M. The mandatory funds are for the procurement of Low Collateral Defeat systems and the
 networking of systems to meet configurations determined by site evaluations.

 The FY 2026 request for Physical Security Equipment includes $166.577 Million of discretionary and $232 Million of mandatory (reconciliation) for a total of $398.577 Million. The mandatory funds Counter Small
 Unmanned Aerial Systems (C-sUAS). Further information for this reconciliation request is provided in Reconciliation Section 20004.

 Footnotes:
 (1)
    FY26 increase ($2.730M) due to the refresh of the Sense and Interdiction (SENSEI) Ashore suite of sensors. Funding will be used to procure new sensors with increased performance to replace the legacy radar and optical sensors
 currently fielded. These new sensors will be integrated within the existing SENSEI Ashore System. This will allow for future expansion to integrate additional defeat capabilities in the areas of C-UAS hardware and software refresh;
 electromagnetic effects testing; and radiation hazards testing to keep pace with the evolving Un-crewed Aircraft System (C-UAS) threat while remaining operationally relevant. Additionally, there is a refresh for Near-Port Security Barriers (N-
 PSBs) beginning in FY26 at the Strategic Weapons Facilities. N-PSBs are floating barriers used to protect vessels, ports, and critical infrastructure from attacks by small, high-speed vessels.
 (2)
       Procurements are executed through Contract POC who establishes pricing based on overall quantity of forecasted buys.
 (3)
       FY26 increase due to increase in quantity and unit price.
 (4)
       FY26 increase due to quantity required to compensate for the increased replacement of armor.
 (5)
       FY26 increase due to quantity required for the increased replacement interval of armor due to deterioration of plates.
 (6)
       FY26 decrease due to quantity required for the increased replacement interval of armor due to deterioration of plates.
 (7)
       FY26 increase due to increase in production and production engineering support for BEACH system.
 (8)
       FY26 to sustain production engineering funding to support production of 8 LROI-COIL systems.
 (9)
       FY26 to sustain LROI-COIL procurements to maintain a consistent industrial base and obtain full operational capability.
 (10)
        Cost increase is standard inflation, did not get the economies of scales cost savings discount due to procuring less units.
 (11)
        Cost increase is standard inflation, procured more in FY26 to make up for decrease in FY25.
 (12)
        FY26 decrease ($6.032M) due to higher Department priorities.
 (13)
        FY26 decrease due to requirement changes.

 Remarks:
 The FY 2026 request for C-UAS includes $29.632M of discretionary and $232M of mandatory (reconciliation) of a total of $261M. The mandatory funds are for the procurement of Low Collateral Defeat systems
 and the networking of systems to meet configurations determined by site evaluations.

 (†)
       indicates the presence of a P-5a

 Footnotes:
 (20)
     The Hand-Held Systems (HHS) being fielded by the Shore program (DroneDefender and DroneBuster) were procured in FY20/FY21 and have been deployed to over 50 operational locations. The current
 inventory is several generations behind the current industry technologies and is in need of a system update/refresh. The fielding of handheld systems has been separated into operational, developmental, and
 system spares. FY26 continues to support phased replacements of Handheld Systems.
 (21)
      Funding provides for the procurement of advanced technologies for customized site-specific capability enhancements to the initial material solution based on localized increased threats. Systems procured
 will be operationally utilized and will be evaluated for inclusion into the Family of Systems baseline. FY26 decrease due to completion of equipment installation at U.S. Central Command (CENTCOM) location
 and test support facilities, and Programmatic focus on CORIAN EW System Technical Refresh in FY26.
 (22)
    Funding provides for installation and infrastructure support for the fielding of procured C-UAS equipment and R2 systems. As well as system upgrades to meet Configuration determined by Site Evaluations.
 FY26 decrease due to completion of the initial material solution and completion of the Alternative Sensors equipment installation at CENTCOM location and test facilities.
 (23)
    The FY26 increase in System Networking funding will enable enhanced support for the integration and networking of R2 systems, ensuring alignment with configuration requirements identified through
 comprehensive site evaluations. This will optimize network performance across operational environments.

 (24)
     FY26 increase in funding will support the procurement of LCD systems essential to enhancing situational awareness and mission readiness. These advanced technologies will provided improved visulization
 and capabilities across operational environments.
 (25)
     Funding provides the field continuation of the initial material solution through the procurement and installation of Ninja technology at remaining Ninja prioritized installation. System procured will provide a
 continuation of the fielding plan, and for providing support in preparation for future Program FOT-MLPF realignment. FY26 decrease due to the completion of the initial material solution.
 (26)
     Congressional FY25 added funding for the continuation of the CORIAN technology through the procurement and installation of an updated hardware to replace a current, aging system reaching end-of-life
 in FY25, in order to maintain mission. Critical components will no longer be able to be replaced or repaired. System procured will provide a continuation of the current technologies, and a roadmap for providing
 support in preparation for future Program DOT-MLPF realignment. FY26 increase supports phased replacement of CORIAN systems.
 (27)
     The FY26 increase in acquisition support will enhance the Navy's ability to execute effective oversight of the acquisition process, enabling timely and cost-effective delivery of program capabilities. The
 associated travel increase supports critical procurement activities such as site visits for system installation oversight, vendor coordination, acceptance testing, and quality assurance inspections, ensuring
 successful fielding of newly acquired systems.
 (28)
     Support equipment (e.g. diagnostic hardware, test assets, manlifts, cranes, towers, and other communication hardware) is used to connect, maintain and/or service C-sUAS layered defense and operational
 capabilities at operational and test facilities, and to continue the upkeep of critical infrastructure. This will result in increased technical support to over 60 locations. FY26 increase supports additional support
 equipment necessary for the phased replacement of the CORIAN systems.
 (29)
     The production engineering support will help to procure new EW hardware and software. Testing will be done prior to the fielding as well as testing once installed. FY26 increase supports additional
 production engineering and testing needed for new CORIAN EW systems.
 (30)
      Procurement and modifications to Allowance Parts Lists (APLs) and Program Support Data (PSD), Packaging, Handling, Storage and Transportation (PHST), manpower, engineering services, advanced
 training capabilities. Funding will be used to support the manufacture of hardware, assembly kits and installation kits. FY26 increase supports additional ILS requirements during the phased replacement of the
 CORIAN systems.
